220		PageEventReviewerReject = "reviewer_reject"
221		// PageEventAdvisoryReviewerReject (#1378, workflow-v0.7): an agent
222		// reject under advisory review authority (agent + human reviewers).
223		// The human approver is the gate, so the reject is arbitrable /
224		// auto-routed — it does not page on its own.
225		PageEventAdvisoryReviewerReject = "advisory_reviewer_reject"
226		// PageEventGatingReviewerReject (#1378, workflow-v0.7): an agent
227		// reject under gating review authority (agent-only review). The
228		// reject blocks and pages the human; this is the class the legacy
229		// bare reviewer_reject resolves to.
230		PageEventGatingReviewerReject   = "gating_reviewer_reject"
231		PageEventPlanRejection          = "plan_rejection"
232		PageEventScopeAmendment         = "scope_amendment"
233		PageEventBudgetOverride         = "budget_override"
234		PageEventPolicyOverride         = "policy_override"
235		PageEventExceptionRequest       = "exception_request"
236		PageEventRequirementArbitration = "requirement_arbitration"
237		// PageEventClarificationRequest (#1057): the planner parked the plan
238		// stage at awaiting_input with a clarification_request because the
239		// issue was not yet plannable. The operator must answer the parked
240		// questions before planning resumes — a judgment a delegation never
241		// absorbs.
242		PageEventClarificationRequest = "clarification_request"
243	)
244	
245	// OperatorAgent holds the delegation knobs for the operator agent
246	// (ADR-040 / #1026). Each may_* knob names the single condition under
247	// which the corresponding action is delegated; an empty knob means
248	// that action is not delegated. MustPageHuman lists events that always
249	// page the human regardless of the knobs. Declared at workflow level
250	// (default) or on an approval gate (override; wins wholesale — knobs
251	// are never merged across levels).
252	type OperatorAgent struct {
253		MayApprove    DelegationCondition `json:"may_approve,omitempty" yaml:"may_approve,omitempty"`
254		MayRouteFixup DelegationCondition `json:"may_route_fixup,omitempty" yaml:"may_route_fixup,omitempty"`
255		MayWaive      DelegationCondition `json:"may_waive,omitempty" yaml:"may_waive,omitempty"`
256		MayRetry      DelegationCondition `json:"may_retry,omitempty" yaml:"may_retry,omitempty"`
257		MayMerge      DelegationCondition `json:"may_merge,omitempty" yaml:"may_merge,omitempty"`
258		MustPageHuman []string            `json:"must_page_human,omitempty" yaml:"must_page_human,omitempty"`
259	}
260	
261	// EffectiveOperatorAgent resolves the operator_agent block that
262	// governs a gate: the gate-level block when present (it wins wholesale
263	// — knobs from the two levels are never merged), else the
264	// workflow-level block, else nil. Nil means fail-closed: nothing is
265	// delegated and every judgment pages the human. g may be nil for
266	// callers evaluating outside any gate context (workflow-level only).
267	func (w *Workflow) EffectiveOperatorAgent(g *Gate) *OperatorAgent {
268		if g != nil && g.OperatorAgent != nil {
269			return g.OperatorAgent
270		}
271		if w != nil {
272			return w.OperatorAgent
273		}
274		return nil
275	}
276	
277	// PeriodicBudget is a workflow-level recurring cost ceiling (ADR-030).
278	// It caps total USD spend across all runs of the workflow within a
279	// calendar period (weekly or monthly), resetting at the period
280	// boundary. Distinct from the per-stage Budget (token/runtime caps on
281	// a single stage execution): a PeriodicBudget governs aggregate spend
282	// across runs, not a single stage's resource use.
283	//
284	// Enforcement reuses the BudgetEnforcement modes:
285	//
286	//   - advisory — a budget_alert audit entry + issue comment fires when
287	//     period spend crosses WarnAt and again at 100%; runs never block.
288	//   - blocking — a NEW run is refused at admission once the calendar
289	//     period's spend exhausts LimitUSD; in-flight runs are untouched
290	//     and an operator can override.
291	//
292	// WarnAt is an optional fraction in [0,1] (e.g. 0.8 for 80%) at which
293	// the advisory warning fires ahead of the 100% crossing. A nil WarnAt
294	// means only the 100% threshold is surfaced.
295	type PeriodicBudget struct {
296		Period      string            `json:"period" yaml:"period"`
297		LimitUSD    float64           `json:"limit_usd" yaml:"limit_usd"`
298		Enforcement BudgetEnforcement `json:"enforcement,omitempty" yaml:"enforcement,omitempty"`
299		WarnAt      *float64          `json:"warn_at,omitempty" yaml:"warn_at,omitempty"`
300	}
301	
302	// Budget period values for PeriodicBudget.Period.
303	const (
304		BudgetPeriodWeekly  = "weekly"
305		BudgetPeriodMonthly = "monthly"
306	)
307	
308	// OnCIFailure is the per-workflow auto-retry policy (#276 / #277).
309	// When set, the dispatcher fires a fresh implement workflow_dispatch

201	// runConcernPayload is one open concern on the wire.
202	type runConcernPayload struct {
203		ID        uuid.UUID `json:"id"`
204		StageKind string    `json:"stage_kind"`
205		Severity  string    `json:"severity"`
206		Category  string    `json:"category"`
207		State     string    `json:"state"`
208		// HasSuggestedPatch reports whether the reviewer attached a mechanical
209		// suggested_patch to this concern (#1165). Only the boolean is
210		// surfaced — the diff text stays elided like the note, keeping the
211		// payload bounded; the fix-up apply path reads the patch server-side.
212		HasSuggestedPatch bool `json:"has_suggested_patch"`
213	}
214	
215	// runDelegationPayload is the operator_agent delegation surface on the
216	// wire (ADR-040 / #1026): one entry per configured may_* knob, each
217	// carrying the named condition and whether current run state satisfies
218	// it, plus the effective block's must_page_human event list. Kept
219	// distinct from delegation.Result per the domain/wire separation the
220	// drive payloads follow.
221	type runDelegationPayload struct {
222		Actions       []runDelegationActionPayload `json:"actions"`
223		MustPageHuman []string                     `json:"must_page_human,omitempty"`
224		// ReviewerRejectClass names the reviewer-reject page-event class the
225		// run currently resolves to (#1378): gating_reviewer_reject (a reject
226		// pages the human) or advisory_reviewer_reject (arbitrable /
227		// auto-routed). Omitted when the implement stage is gateless — no
228		// agent-reviewer authority — preserving byte-identical responses for
229		// runs that resolve gateless.
230		ReviewerRejectClass string `json:"reviewer_reject_class,omitempty"`
231	}
232	
233	// runDelegationActionPayload is one knob's evaluation on the wire.
234	// unmet_reason names the exact failed predicate when met is false.
235	type runDelegationActionPayload struct {
236		Action      string `json:"action"`
237		Condition   string `json:"condition"`
238		Met         bool   `json:"met"`
239		UnmetReason string `json:"unmet_reason,omitempty"`
240	}
241	
242	// issueContextPayload mirrors run.IssueContext on the wire. Kept
243	// distinct from the domain type so a future field change in the
244	// store doesn't accidentally leak through the API surface.
245	type issueContextPayload struct {
246		Title    string                `json:"title"`
247		Body     string                `json:"body"`
248		URL      string                `json:"url"`
249		Number   int                   `json:"number"`
250		Comments []issueCommentPayload `json:"comments,omitempty"`
251	}

258	type issueCommentPayload struct {

# operator_agent delegation knobs (ADR-040 / #1026, version 0.5).
# Exercises both placement levels: a workflow-level default block and
# a per-gate override on the plan stage's approval gate. The implement
# stage's gate carries no block, so it falls back to the workflow
# default (EffectiveOperatorAgent precedence).
version: "0.5"
roles:
  founder:
    members: ["@kuhlman-labs"]
workflows:
  feature_change:
    description: "Delegation knobs at workflow level with a per-gate override."
    operator_agent:
      may_approve: clean_dual_approval
      may_route_fixup: convergent_concerns
      may_retry: infra_flake
      may_merge: gates_resolved_ci_green
      must_page_human: [reviewer_reject, budget_override]
    stages:
      - id: plan
        type: plan
        executor:
          agent: claude-code
        produces:
          - artifact: plan
            schema: standard_v1
        gates:
          - type: approval
            approvers:
              any_of: [founder]
            operator_agent:
              may_approve: clean_dual_approval
              may_waive: solo_low
              must_page_human:
                - plan_rejection
                - scope_amendment
                - policy_override
                - exception_request
                - requirement_arbitration
      - id: implement
        type: implement
        executor:
          agent: claude-code
        produces:
          - artifact: pull_request
        gates:
          - type: approval
            approvers:
              any_of: [founder]

428	    "operator_agent": {
429	      "type": "object",
430	      "additionalProperties": false,
431	      "description": "Delegation knobs for the operator agent (ADR-040 / #1026). Each may_* knob names the single backend-evaluable condition under which the operator agent may take that action without paging the human; the per-knob enums are a closed set — the backend must be able to answer every condition from run state. Absent block (and absent knob) = fail-closed: nothing is delegated, every judgment pages the human. Declarable at workflow level (default for all gates) and on an approval gate (per-gate override; a gate-level block wins WHOLESALE over the workflow block — knobs are not merged). Authority semantics (ADR-027) are unchanged: delegation changes who may act, not what gates exist. Requires version 0.5+.",
432	      "properties": {
433	        "may_approve": {
434	          "type": "string",
435	          "enum": ["clean_dual_approval"],
436	          "description": "The operator agent may approve a gate when every configured reviewer for the gated stage returned an approve verdict and zero concerns are open."
437	        },
438	        "may_route_fixup": {
439	          "type": "string",
440	          "enum": ["convergent_concerns"],
441	          "description": "The operator agent may route review concerns to a fixup when all reviewer verdicts are in, at least one concern is open, and no reviewer rejected."
442	        },
443	        "may_waive": {
444	          "type": "string",
445	          "enum": ["solo_low"],
446	          "description": "The operator agent may waive a concern when it is the only open concern and its severity is low."
447	        },
448	        "may_retry": {
449	          "type": "string",
450	          "enum": ["infra_flake"],
451	          "description": "The operator agent may retry a failed stage when the latest stage failure is classified as an infrastructure flake."
452	        },
453	        "may_merge": {
454	          "type": "string",
455	          "enum": ["gates_resolved_ci_green"],
456	          "description": "The operator agent may merge when no gate approvals are pending, zero concerns are open, the PR is open, and required checks are green. Evaluated and surfaced in v0; merge itself happens on GitHub, so backend enforcement attaches once a merge action surface exists."
457	        },
458	        "must_page_human": {
459	          "type": "array",
460	          "uniqueItems": true,
461	          "description": "Events that always page the human regardless of the may_* knobs. Closed v0 set; an event listed here is never absorbed by a delegation. The reviewer-reject taxonomy carries three tokens: the explicit advisory_reviewer_reject (an agent reject under advisory review authority — arbitrable / auto-routed) and gating_reviewer_reject (an agent reject under gating authority — pages the human), plus the legacy bare reviewer_reject, which is preserved and maps to the gating class for back-compat (requires version 0.7+ for the explicit tokens).",
462	          "items": {
463	            "type": "string",
464	            "enum": [
465	              "reviewer_reject",
466	              "advisory_reviewer_reject",
467	              "gating_reviewer_reject",
468	              "plan_rejection",
469	              "scope_amendment",
470	              "budget_override",
471	              "policy_override",
472	              "exception_request",
473	              "requirement_arbitration",
474	              "clarification_request"
475	            ]
476	          }
477	        }
478	      }
479	    },
480	
481	    "approvers": {
482	      "type": "object",
483	      "description": "Either any_of (one approver suffices) or all_of (every named role must approve).",
484	      "additionalProperties": false,
485	      "minProperties": 1,
486	      "maxProperties": 1,
487	      "properties": {
488	        "any_of": {
489	          "type": "array",
490	          "minItems": 1,
491	          "uniqueItems": true,
492	          "items": { "type": "string", "pattern": "^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$" }
493	        },
494	        "all_of": {
495	          "type": "array",
496	          "minItems": 1,
497	          "uniqueItems": true,
498	          "items": { "type": "string", "pattern": "^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$" }
499	        }
500	      }
501	    },
502	
503	    "reviewers_config": {
504	      "type": "object",
505	      "additionalProperties": false,
506	      "description": "Plan-review reviewer counts for plan stages (ADR-027). Authority table: agent>0 && human==0 → gating (agent rejections block stage advancement); agent>0 && human>0 → advisory (agent verdicts surfaced, cannot block human approval); agent==0 → gateless. The effective agent count is len(agents) when the `agents` list is present and non-empty, else the bare `agent` integer. When the reviewers block is absent entirely, the backend defaults to {human:1} — preserving pre-ADR-027 behavior.",
507	      "properties": {
508	        "agent": {
509	          "type": "integer",
510	          "minimum": 0,
511	          "description": "Number of agent reviewers to invoke after the plan artifact is validated. 0 (default) means no agent review. Superseded by `agents` when that list is present and non-empty; each invocation then uses the deployment's precedence-selected default adapter."
512	        },
513	        "agents": {
514	          "type": "array",
515	          "minItems": 1,
516	          "description": "Heterogeneous agent reviewers (#955): one entry per reviewer invocation, each naming its provider and optionally its model. When present and non-empty this list supersedes the bare `agent` integer count — the effective agent count for the ADR-027 authority table is len(agents). Authority semantics are unchanged: heterogeneity changes WHO reviews, not gating semantics.",

# operator_agent delegation knobs (ADR-040 / #1026, version 0.5).
# Exercises both placement levels: a workflow-level default block and
# a per-gate override on the plan stage's approval gate. The implement
# stage's gate carries no block, so it falls back to the workflow
# default (EffectiveOperatorAgent precedence).
version: "0.5"
roles:
  founder:
    members: ["@kuhlman-labs"]
workflows:
  feature_change:
    description: "Delegation knobs at workflow level with a per-gate override."
    operator_agent:
      may_approve: clean_dual_approval
      may_route_fixup: convergent_concerns
      may_retry: infra_flake
      may_merge: gates_resolved_ci_green
      must_page_human: [reviewer_reject, budget_override]
    stages:
      - id: plan
        type: plan
        executor:
          agent: claude-code
        produces:
          - artifact: plan
            schema: standard_v1
        gates:
          - type: approval
            approvers:
              any_of: [founder]
            operator_agent:
              may_approve: clean_dual_approval
              may_waive: solo_low
              must_page_human:
                - plan_rejection
                - scope_amendment
                - policy_override
                - exception_request
                - requirement_arbitration
      - id: implement
        type: implement
        executor:
          agent: claude-code
        produces:
          - artifact: pull_request
        gates:
          - type: approval
            approvers:
              any_of: [founder]

1306	// newDelegationServer wires the run + audit + concern fakes the

1308	// ListStagesForRun the base fakeRepo deliberately errors on.
1309	func newDelegationServer(t *testing.T) (*Server, *driveE2ERepo, *auditFake, *fakeConcernRepo) {
1310		t.Helper()
1311		repo := &driveE2ERepo{fakeRepo: newFakeRepo()}
1312		au := newAuditFake()
1313		cr := newFakeConcernRepo()
1314		s := New(Config{Addr: "127.0.0.1:0", RunRepo: repo, AuditRepo: au, ConcernRepo: cr})
1315		return s, repo, au, cr
1316	}
1317	
1318	// seedReviewEntry appends one payload-carrying review audit entry to
1319	// the fake's seeded history at the given per-run sequence.
1320	func seedReviewEntry(t *testing.T, au *auditFake, runID uuid.UUID, seq int64, category string, payload any) {
1321		t.Helper()
1322		b, err := json.Marshal(payload)
1323		if err != nil {
1324			t.Fatalf("marshal %s payload: %v", category, err)
1325		}
1326		rid := runID
1327		au.seeded = append(au.seeded, &audit.Entry{
1328			RunID: &rid, Sequence: seq, Category: category,
1329			Payload: b, Timestamp: time.Now().UTC(),
1330		})
1331	}
1332	
1333	// delegationAction returns the named action's entry from the response's
1334	// delegation block.
1335	func delegationAction(t *testing.T, resp runResponse, action string) runDelegationActionPayload {
1336		t.Helper()
1337		if resp.Delegation == nil {
1338			t.Fatal("delegation block missing")
1339		}
1340		for _, a := range resp.Delegation.Actions {
1341			if a.Action == action {
1342				return a
1343			}
1344		}
1345		t.Fatalf("no %q action in delegation block: %+v", action, resp.Delegation.Actions)
1346		return runDelegationActionPayload{}
1347	}
1348

7:### Schema evolution policy
17:## Top-level shape
20:version: "0.3" # required; "0.3", "0.4" (adds workflow.budgets), "0.5" (adds operator_agent), "0.6" (adds workflow.decomposition.max_parallel), or "0.7" (adds the explicit advisory_reviewer_reject / gating_reviewer_reject page-event classes)
32:    operator_agent: {...} # optional; delegation knobs for the operator agent (ADR-040, v0.5+)
40:## Stages
47:    model: claude-opus-4-8    # optional; per-stage model override (see ### Executor model override)
49:    verify:                   # optional; in-band test gate (see ### Verify gate)
53:    agent_self_retry: false   # optional; opt-in per ADR-023 (see ### Agent self-retry)
61:  reviewers: # optional; plan-review agents and human counts (ADR-027, see ### Plan reviewers)
68:### Executor agent (provider)
74:### Verify gate
95:### Agent self-retry
108:### Executor model override
123:### Plan reviewers
164:## Inputs
178:## Produces
191:### `originating_issue` + `rendered_comment` — plan-review surface (ADR-020 / #321)
201:## Constraints (implement stages)
221:## Budget (per-stage)
234:## Periodic budgets (workflow-level, v0.4+)
260:## Gates
270:  operator_agent: {...} # optional; per-gate delegation override (ADR-040, v0.5+; see ## Operator agent delegation)
300:## Agent timeouts (v0.3 additions, #452)
336:## On CI failure (auto-retry)
353:## Drive mode
368:## Operator agent delegation (v0.5+)
375:    operator_agent: # workflow-level default for every gate
376:      may_approve: clean_dual_approval
381:      must_page_human: [reviewer_reject, budget_override]
392:            operator_agent: # per-gate override; wins WHOLESALE over the workflow block
393:              may_approve: clean_dual_approval
400:| `may_approve` | `clean_dual_approval` | every configured reviewer for the gated stage returned an approve verdict AND zero concerns are open |
406:**Fail-closed default.** An absent `operator_agent` block delegates nothing: every judgment pages the human, exactly as before v0.5. Likewise an absent knob within a block — only the named knobs are delegated. Specs without the block behave byte-identically to today.
408:**Precedence.** A gate-level block (approval gates only — the schema rejects `operator_agent` on `check` gates) overrides the workflow-level block **wholesale**: knobs are never merged across levels, so a gate block that omits `may_retry` does not inherit the workflow's `may_retry`. Resolution lives in `spec.Workflow.EffectiveOperatorAgent(gate)`: gate block if present, else workflow block, else nil.
410:**`must_page_human`.** Events that always page the human regardless of the `may_*` knobs (closed set: `reviewer_reject`, `advisory_reviewer_reject`, `gating_reviewer_reject`, `plan_rejection`, `scope_amendment`, `budget_override`, `policy_override`, `exception_request`, `requirement_arbitration`, `clarification_request`). An event listed here is never absorbed by a delegation. (`clarification_request` is the planner parking the plan stage at `awaiting_input` because the issue is not yet plannable — #1057.)
420:**Decision-class taxonomy.** Each class of operator decision resolves to either an **auto** action (delegated via a `may_*` knob when its condition is met) or a **page** (a `must_page_human` event). The operator agent reads this mapping from config rather than judging the advisory-vs-hard line per-run.
424:| Clean dual approval (all verdicts approve, no concerns) | auto | `may_approve` / `clean_dual_approval` |
434:## Decomposition controls (v0.6+)
451:### Notes — soak window
455:## Test conventions (#1004)
482:## Identifier namespaces
488:| `version`                   | `"0.3"` \| `"0.4"` \| `"0.5"` \| `"0.6"` \| `"0.7"`                          | 0.7 adds the explicit `advisory_reviewer_reject` / `gating_reviewer_reject` page-event classes (#1378); 0.6 adds `decomposition.max_parallel` (#1146); 0.5 adds `operator_agent` (ADR-040 / #1026); 0.4 adds workflow-level `budgets` (ADR-030 / #688); 0.3 adds `on_ci_failure.max_retries` (#277); 0.2 dropped `blocking_checks` |
511:| `operator_agent.may_*`      | one closed condition per knob (see ## Operator agent delegation)             | v0.5+; workflow level + approval-gate override (gate wins wholesale); absent → fail-closed          |
512:| `operator_agent.must_page_human` | `reviewer_reject`, `advisory_reviewer_reject`, `gating_reviewer_reject`, `plan_rejection`, `scope_amendment`, `budget_override`, `policy_override`, `exception_request`, `requirement_arbitration`, `clarification_request` | closed set; always pages the human regardless of `may_*` knobs. The explicit reject classes (v0.7+) make the taxonomy self-documenting; legacy `reviewer_reject` resolves to the gating sense |
514:## Validation rules beyond the schema

content.text: Let me read the Operator agent delegation section in detail to place model_policy docs:

content.content:
368	## Operator agent delegation (v0.5+)
369	
370	Delegation knobs for the operator agent (ADR-040 / #1026). Each `may_*` knob names the **single backend-evaluable condition** under which the operator agent may take that action without paging the human. The per-knob values are closed single-entry enums in v0 — a condition exists only if the backend can answer it from run state.
371	
372	```yaml
373	workflows:
374	  feature_change:
375	    operator_agent: # workflow-level default for every gate
376	      may_approve: clean_dual_approval
377	      may_route_fixup: convergent_concerns
378	      may_waive: solo_low
379	      may_retry: infra_flake
380	      may_merge: gates_resolved_ci_green
381	      must_page_human: [reviewer_reject, budget_override]
382	    stages:
383	      - id: plan
384	        type: plan
385	        executor: { agent: claude-code }
386	        produces:
387	          - artifact: plan
388	            schema: standard_v1
389	        gates:
390	          - type: approval
391	            approvers: { any_of: [founder] }
392	            operator_agent: # per-gate override; wins WHOLESALE over the workflow block
393	              may_approve: clean_dual_approval
394	```
395	
396	**Per-knob conditions** (closed v0 set):
397	
398	| Knob | Condition | Met when |
399	|---|---|---|
400	| `may_approve` | `clean_dual_approval` | every configured reviewer for the gated stage returned an approve verdict AND zero concerns are open |
401	| `may_route_fixup` | `convergent_concerns` | all reviewer verdicts are in, at least one concern is open, and no **gating-authority** reviewer rejected — under advisory authority (agent + human reviewers, ADR-027) an agent reject is arbitrable and does NOT disqualify; under gating authority (agent-only) a reject fires `reviewer_reject` and pages |
402	| `may_waive` | `solo_low` | exactly one open concern and its severity is low |
403	| `may_retry` | `infra_flake` | the latest stage failure is classified as an infrastructure flake |
404	| `may_merge` | `gates_resolved_ci_green` | no pending gate approvals, zero open concerns, PR open, required checks green |
405	
406	**Fail-closed default.** An absent `operator_agent` block delegates nothing: every judgment pages the human, exactly as before v0.5. Likewise an absent knob within a block — only the named knobs are delegated. Specs without the block behave byte-identically to today.
407	
408	**Precedence.** A gate-level block (approval gates only — the schema rejects `operator_agent` on `check` gates) overrides the workflow-level block **wholesale**: knobs are never merged across levels, so a gate block that omits `may_retry` does not inherit the workflow's `may_retry`. Resolution lives in `spec.Workflow.EffectiveOperatorAgent(gate)`: gate block if present, else workflow block, else nil.
409	
410	**`must_page_human`.** Events that always page the human regardless of the `may_*` knobs (closed set: `reviewer_reject`, `advisory_reviewer_reject`, `gating_reviewer_reject`, `plan_rejection`, `scope_amendment`, `budget_override`, `policy_override`, `exception_request`, `requirement_arbitration`, `clarification_request`). An event listed here is never absorbed by a delegation. (`clarification_request` is the planner parking the plan stage at `awaiting_input` because the issue is not yet plannable — #1057.)
411	
412	**Reviewer-reject taxonomy (v0.7+).** The reviewer-reject class is now self-documenting via two explicit tokens (#1378): `gating_reviewer_reject` — an agent reject on an agent-only implement review, where no human approver overrides it (ADR-027); this **pages** the human — and `advisory_reviewer_reject` — an agent reject under agent + human authority; this is non-blocking and **arbitrable** via `may_route_fixup` / `convergent_concerns`, so it does not page on its own. The legacy bare `reviewer_reject` is preserved and **resolves to the gating sense** for back-compat: a spec using it pages exactly as before. The page/auto decision itself stays resolved from ADR-027 review authority (`planreview.ResolveAuthority`) — the explicit tokens only make the resolved class legible in config; they do not change behavior. A **human** reviewer reject does not arrive as an `implement_reviewed` verdict at all — it surfaces as `plan_rejection` / gate rejection, which already pages.
413	
414	The class a run currently resolves to is surfaced on the wire as `reviewer_reject_class` on the `GET /v0/runs/{id}` delegation block (`gating_reviewer_reject` / `advisory_reviewer_reject`, omitted when the implement stage is gateless), so a reader need not cross-reference the authority resolver.
415	
416	**Authority unchanged (ADR-027).** Delegation changes *who* may act at a gate, not what gates exist or how reviewer authority resolves. The condition evaluator reads authority from the same ADR-027 mechanism the review pipeline uses (`planreview.ResolveAuthority`: advisory when agent + human, gating when agent-only), so a delegated decision can never weaken a gating gate — it only loosens delegation where ADR-027 already makes agent verdicts non-blocking.
417	
418	- `may_merge` is evaluated and surfaced but has no backend merge endpoint to enforce in v0 — merge happens on GitHub; enforcement attaches when a merge action surface exists.
419	
420	**Decision-class taxonomy.** Each class of operator decision resolves to either an **auto** action (delegated via a `may_*` knob when its condition is met) or a **page** (a `must_page_human` event). The operator agent reads this mapping from config rather than judging the advisory-vs-hard line per-run.
421	
422	| Decision class | Resolution | Via |
423	|---|---|---|
424	| Clean dual approval (all verdicts approve, no concerns) | auto | `may_approve` / `clean_dual_approval` |
425	| Advisory-concern arbitration (verdicts in, no gating reject, concern open) | auto | `may_route_fixup` / `convergent_concerns` |
426	| Advisory reviewer reject (agent reject under agent + human authority) | auto | `may_route_fixup` / `convergent_concerns` (arbitrable; non-blocking per ADR-027); legible token `advisory_reviewer_reject` |
427	| Gating reviewer reject (agent reject under agent-only authority) | page | `gating_reviewer_reject` (legacy `reviewer_reject` resolves to this sense) |
428	| Human / hard reject | page | `plan_rejection` (gate rejection — never an `implement_reviewed` verdict) |
429	| Scope amendment (agent requests new in-scope paths) | page | `scope_amendment` |
430	| Budget / policy override | page | `budget_override`, `policy_override` |
431	| Plan design-fork / clarification gate (plan parked at `awaiting_input`) | page | `clarification_request`, `requirement_arbitration`, `exception_request` |
432	| Merge (gates resolved, CI green) | auto | `may_merge` / `gates_resolved_ci_green` (surfaced-only in v0; no enforce endpoint) |
433

file.content:
## Operator agent delegation (v0.5+)

Delegation knobs for the operator agent (ADR-040 / #1026). Each `may_*` knob names the **single backend-evaluable condition** under which the operator agent may take that action without paging the human. The per-knob values are closed single-entry enums in v0 — a condition exists only if the backend can answer it from run state.

```yaml
workflows:
  feature_change:
    operator_agent: # workflow-level default for every gate
      may_approve: clean_dual_approval
      may_route_fixup: convergent_concerns
      may_waive: solo_low
      may_retry: infra_flake
      may_merge: gates_resolved_ci_green
      must_page_human: [reviewer_reject, budget_override]
    stages:
      - id: plan
        type: plan
        executor: { agent: claude-code }
        produces:
          - artifact: plan
            schema: standard_v1
        gates:
          - type: approval
            approvers: { any_of: [founder] }
            operator_agent: # per-gate override; wins WHOLESALE over the workflow block
              may_approve: clean_dual_approval
```

**Per-knob conditions** (closed v0 set):

| Knob | Condition | Met when |
|---|---|---|
| `may_approve` | `clean_dual_approval` | every configured reviewer for the gated stage returned an approve verdict AND zero concerns are open |
| `may_route_fixup` | `convergent_concerns` | all reviewer verdicts are in, at least one concern is open, and no **gating-authority** reviewer rejected — under advisory authority (agent + human reviewers, ADR-027) an agent reject is arbitrable and does NOT disqualify; under gating authority (agent-only) a reject fires `reviewer_reject` and pages |
| `may_waive` | `solo_low` | exactly one open concern and its severity is low |
| `may_retry` | `infra_flake` | the latest stage failure is classified as an infrastructure flake |
| `may_merge` | `gates_resolved_ci_green` | no pending gate approvals, zero open concerns, PR open, required checks green |

**Fail-closed default.** An absent `operator_agent` block delegates nothing: every judgment pages the human, exactly as before v0.5. Likewise an absent knob within a block — only the named knobs are delegated. Specs without the block behave byte-identically to today.

**Precedence.** A gate-level block (approval gates only — the schema rejects `operator_agent` on `check` gates) overrides the workflow-level block **wholesale**: knobs are never merged across levels, so a gate block that omits `may_retry` does not inherit the workflow's `may_retry`. Resolution lives in `spec.Workflow.EffectiveOperatorAgent(gate)`: gate block if present, else workflow block, else nil.

**`must_page_human`.** Events that always page the human regardless of the `may_*` knobs (closed set: `reviewer_reject`, `advisory_reviewer_reject`, `gating_reviewer_reject`, `plan_rejection`, `scope_amendment`, `budget_override`, `policy_override`, `exception_request`, `requirement_arbitration`, `clarification_request`). An event listed here is never absorbed by a delegation. (`clarification_request` is the planner parking the plan stage at `awaiting_input` because the issue is not yet plannable — #1057.)

**Reviewer-reject taxonomy (v0.7+).** The reviewer-reject class is now self-documenting via two explicit tokens (#1378): `gating_reviewer_reject` — an agent reject on an agent-only implement review, where no human approver overrides it (ADR-027); this **pages** the human — and `advisory_reviewer_reject` — an agent reject under agent + human authority; this is non-blocking and **arbitrable** via `may_route_fixup` / `convergent_concerns`, so it does not page on its own. The legacy bare `reviewer_reject` is preserved and **resolves to the gating sense** for back-compat: a spec using it pages exactly as before. The page/auto decision itself stays resolved from ADR-027 review authority (`planreview.ResolveAuthority`) — the explicit tokens only make the resolved class legible in config; they do not change behavior. A **human** reviewer reject does not arrive as an `implement_reviewed` verdict at all — it surfaces as `plan_rejection` / gate rejection, which already pages.

The class a run currently resolves to is surfaced on the wire as `reviewer_reject_class` on the `GET /v0/runs/{id}` delegation block (`gating_reviewer_reject` / `advisory_reviewer_reject`, omitted when the implement stage is gateless), so a reader need not cross-reference the authority resolver.

**Authority unchanged (ADR-027).** Delegation changes *who* may act at a gate, not what gates exist or how reviewer authority resolves. The condition evaluator reads authority from the same ADR-027 mechanism the review pipeline uses (`planreview.ResolveAuthority`: advisory when agent + human, gating when agent-only), so a delegated decision can never weaken a gating gate — it only loosens delegation where ADR-027 already makes agent verdicts non-blocking.

- `may_merge` is evaluated and surfaced but has no backend merge endpoint to enforce in v0 — merge happens on GitHub; enforcement attaches when a merge action surface exists.

**Decision-class taxonomy.** Each class of operator decision resolves to either an **auto** action (delegated via a `may_*` knob when its condition is met) or a **page** (a `must_page_human` event). The operator agent reads this mapping from config rather than judging the advisory-vs-hard line per-run.

| Decision class | Resolution | Via |
|---|---|---|
| Clean dual approval (all verdicts approve, no concerns) | auto | `may_approve` / `clean_dual_approval` |
| Advisory-concern arbitration (verdicts in, no gating reject, concern open) | auto | `may_route_fixup` / `convergent_concerns` |
| Advisory reviewer reject (agent reject under agent + human authority) | auto | `may_route_fixup` / `convergent_concerns` (arbitrable; non-blocking per ADR-027); legible token `advisory_reviewer_reject` |
| Gating reviewer reject (agent reject under agent-only authority) | page | `gating_reviewer_reject` (legacy `reviewer_reject` resolves to this sense) |
| Human / hard reject | page | `plan_rejection` (gate rejection — never an `implement_reviewed` verdict) |
| Scope amendment (agent requests new in-scope paths) | page | `scope_amendment` |
| Budget / policy override | page | `budget_override`, `policy_override` |
| Plan design-fork / clarification gate (plan parked at `awaiting_input`) | page | `clarification_request`, `requirement_arbitration`, `exception_request` |
| Merge (gates resolved, CI green) | auto | `may_merge` / `gates_resolved_ci_green` (surfaced-only in v0; no enforce endpoint) |

content.text: I'll add a model_policy subsection to workflow-v0.md after the decision-class table:

input.old_string: | Merge (gates resolved, CI green) | auto | `may_merge` / `gates_resolved_ci_green` (surfaced-only in v0; no enforce endpoint) |
input.new_string:
| Merge (gates resolved, CI green) | auto | `may_merge` / `gates_resolved_ci_green` (surfaced-only in v0; no enforce endpoint) |

**`model_policy` — operator-agent model selection (#1421).** An optional object on the `operator_agent` block declaring how the operator agent picks each stage's model (scenario A: an operator agent pinned to a frontier model decides each stage's model). Spec-declared and per-repo configurable rather than left to ad-hoc per-gate overrides.

```yaml
operator_agent:
  model_policy:
    strategy: explicit_defaults     # follow_plan_recommendation | explicit_defaults
    defaults:                        # applied under explicit_defaults; each stage optional
      plan: claude-opus-4-8
      implement: claude-sonnet-4-6
      review: gpt-5.5
    allowed:                         # composes with — never widens — the deployment allow-list
      - claude-opus-4-8
      - claude-sonnet-4-6
      - gpt-5.5
```

- **`strategy`** — `follow_plan_recommendation` (the operator agent follows the plan artifact's per-stage model recommendation) or `explicit_defaults` (it applies the `defaults` map, falling back to the deployment default for any unset stage).
- **`defaults`** — optional per-stage model (`plan` / `implement` / `review`), each optional.
- **`allowed`** — the models the operator agent may select; it **composes with, never widens,** the deployment per-adapter allow-list.

**Declarative only.** `model_policy` adds **no** new backend resolution code in v0 — the existing `resolvePlanModel` / `resolveImplementModel` / `resolveReviewModel` ladders are untouched. The operator agent **reads** the resolved policy from the `GET /v0/runs/{id}` delegation block (surfaced as `delegation.model_policy`) and **applies** it through the existing per-stage model override channels (#1416), still bounded by the deployment allow-list. An absent `model_policy` is byte-identical to today (omitted from the wire). Requires version `0.5+`.

**Precedence.** `model_policy` is part of the `operator_agent` block, so it inherits the **same wholesale-override semantics** as the `may_*` knobs: a gate-level `operator_agent` block replaces the workflow-level one entirely — `model_policy` is never merged across levels.
