          type: [string, "null"]
          format: date-time

  responses:
    BadRequest:
      description: Request body or query parameters were invalid.
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
    Unauthorized:
      description: |
        Authentication missing or invalid (`error: unauthenticated`).
        Note: a credential that fails to validate because the database
        was unreachable surfaces as `503 service_unavailable` at the
        auth boundary, NOT as 401 — a 401 here means the credential is
        absent or genuinely bad on a healthy database (#764).
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
    ServiceUnavailable:
      description: |
        A dependency required to serve the request was unavailable.
        At the auth boundary (`error: service_unavailable`, message
        `database unavailable; retry shortly`) this means the database
        could not be reached to validate the presented credential —
        distinct from a bad credential (401) on a healthy database
        (#764). Transient; retry with backoff.
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
    Forbidden:
      description: |
        Authenticated identity lacks permission for the operation
        (`error: forbidden`), or — on state-changing methods from a
        session-cookie identity — the `X-CSRF-Token` header didn't
        match the `__Host-csrf` cookie (`error: csrf_required`).
        Bearer-token clients bypass the CSRF check.
        Bearer tokens that authenticate successfully but lack a required
        write scope receive `error: insufficient_scope` with HTTP 403.
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
    NotFound:
      description: Referenced resource does not exist.
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
    Conflict:
      description: Request conflicts with current resource state.
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Default security: cookie OR bearer for all UI/CLI endpoints. Runner-
# facing and webhook endpoints override this individually.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
security:
  - sessionCookie: []
  - bearerToken: []

paths:
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Liveness — public, unversioned
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  /healthz:
    get:
      operationId: getHealthz
      summary: Liveness probe
      description: |
        Lightweight check used by load balancers, ECS health checks, and
        local smoke tests. Returns build version so operators can confirm
        a deploy reached the instance. Implemented in
        `backend/internal/server/handlers.go`.
      security: []
      tags: [diagnostics]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Process is up.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                required: [status, version, git_sha, min_runner_version, schemas]
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
                  version:
                    type: string
                    example: dev
                  git_sha:
                    type: string
                    description: |
                      Git commit SHA the binary was built from. scripts/dev
                      stamps the short HEAD SHA into dev builds, with a
                      "-dirty" suffix when the tree had uncommitted changes at
                      build time. "unknown" only for unstamped builds (built
                      outside scripts/dev without -ldflags). Operators use
                      this to confirm a deploy matches the expected commit.
                    example: abc1234
                  min_runner_version:
                    type: string
                    description: |
                      Minimum fishhawk-runner version required to interoperate
                      with this backend. "dev" signals no enforcement (local dev).
                      Runners older than this value should exit rather than proceed.
                    example: dev
                  schemas:
                    type: object
                    description: |
                      Map of schema name to hex-encoded SHA-256 of the backend's
                      embedded canonical schema bytes. Keys: "plan-standard-v1",
                      "workflow-v0". Callers compare against their own embedded
                      hashes to detect schema drift.
                    additionalProperties:
                      type: string
                    example:
                      plan-standard-v1: "abc123..."
                      workflow-v0: "def456..."
                  start_nonce:
                    type: string
                    description: |
                      Per-start opaque identity token, echoed verbatim from the
                      operator-supplied FISHHAWKD_START_NONCE env var /
                      --start-nonce flag. Present only when the operator set
                      one; omitted otherwise. scripts/dev generates a nonce per
                      spawn and requires it to round-trip here, proving the
                      listener on the port is the daemon it started even
                      across OS pid reuse.
                    example: "9f86d081884c7d65"
        '503':
          description: Process is shutting down or otherwise not ready.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Auth — sign in via GitHub OAuth, manage scoped tokens
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  /v0/auth/github/login:
    get:
      operationId: githubLogin
      summary: Begin GitHub OAuth sign-in
      description: |
        Redirects the browser to GitHub's OAuth authorize endpoint with a
        backend-generated state value. The state is bound to the browser
        via a temporary cookie and verified on callback.
      security: []
      tags: [auth]
      responses:
        '302':
          description: Redirect to GitHub's OAuth authorize URL.
        '500':
          description: OAuth state could not be issued.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/auth/github/callback:
    get:
      operationId: githubCallback
      summary: GitHub OAuth callback
      description: |
        Verifies the state, exchanges the code for a GitHub access token,
        looks up or creates the user, and sets the `fishhawk_session`
        cookie. Redirects to the SPA on success.
      security: []
      tags: [auth]
      parameters:
        - name: code
          in: query
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: state
          in: query
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '302':
          description: Redirect to the SPA root with session cookie set.
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest' }

  /v0/auth/github/manifest-flow-start:
    get:
      operationId: githubManifestFlowStart
      summary: Begin GitHub App manifest registration
      description: |
        Mints a manifest-flow state value, sets it in a short-lived
        cookie, and renders an HTML page with a self-submitting form
        that POSTs the App manifest to GitHub. After the operator
        confirms the App's name on GitHub's side, GitHub redirects
        back to `/v0/auth/github/manifest-callback` with a one-shot
        `code`.

        Designed to be opened directly in a browser; the response is
        HTML, not JSON.
      security: []
      tags: [auth]
      parameters:
        - name: backend_url
          in: query
          required: true
          description: Absolute base URL of fishhawkd (e.g. `http://localhost:8080`).
          schema: { type: string, format: uri }
        - name: webhook_url
          in: query
          required: true
          description: |
            Destination GitHub will deliver webhooks to. For local dev
            this is typically a smee.io forwarding URL since GitHub
            cannot reach `localhost`.
          schema: { type: string, format: uri }
        - name: owner
          in: query
          description: |
            GitHub user or org login. When set, the form posts to
            `https://github.com/organizations/<owner>/settings/apps/new`
            instead of the operator's personal account.
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: name
          in: query
          description: App name override. Defaults to "Fishhawk".
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML form that auto-submits to GitHub.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema: { type: string }
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest' }
        '503':
          description: Manifest flow not configured on this backend.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/auth/github/manifest-callback:
    get:
      operationId: githubManifestCallback
      summary: Receive GitHub App manifest-flow callback
      description: |
        Verifies the manifest-flow state, exchanges the one-shot
        `code` with `https://api.github.com/app-manifests/{code}/conversions`,
        and renders an HTML success page that displays the freshly
        minted App ID, OAuth client ID + secret, webhook secret, and
        PEM private key once. The backend does not persist these —
        the operator copies them out into `.env` (local dev) or the
        configured secrets backend (hosted, deferred).

        The conversion code is single-use and good for ten minutes;
        on `502` the operator must restart from
        `/v0/auth/github/manifest-flow-start`.
      security: []
      tags: [auth]
      parameters:
        - name: code
          in: query
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: state
          in: query
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML success page with one-time-visible credentials.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema: { type: string }
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest' }
        '502':
          description: GitHub rejected the conversion code (expired, reused, or invalid).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503':
          description: Manifest flow not configured on this backend.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/auth/me:
    get:
      operationId: getMe
      summary: Current user
      description: Returns the authenticated user, or 401 if unauthenticated.
      tags: [auth]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: The authenticated user.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/User' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }

  /v0/auth/logout:
    post:
      operationId: logout
      summary: Sign out
      description: Invalidates the current session.
      tags: [auth]
      responses:
        '204':
          description: Session destroyed.
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden' }

  /v0/tokens:
    get:
      operationId: listTokens
      summary: List API tokens for the current user
      tags: [auth]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Active tokens. Token strings are NOT returned.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                allOf:
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Pagination'
                  - type: object
                    properties:
                      items:
                        type: array
                        items: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ApiToken' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
    post:
      operationId: createToken
      summary: Mint a new scoped API token
      description: |
        Returns the freshly-minted token string ONCE in the response body.
        The token is not retrievable later; if lost, revoke and re-issue.
      tags: [auth]
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [scopes]
              properties:
                scopes:
                  type: array
                  items: { type: string }
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Token created. The plaintext value is in the response.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                allOf:
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/ApiToken'
                  - type: object
                    required: [token]
                    properties:
                      token:
                        type: string
                        description: |
                          The bearer token. Store immediately — not
                          retrievable later.
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden' }

  /v0/tokens/{token_id}:
    delete:
      operationId: revokeToken
      summary: Revoke an API token
      tags: [auth]
      parameters:
        - { name: token_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '204':
          description: Token revoked.
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Runs
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  /v0/runs:
    get:
      operationId: listRuns
      summary: List workflow runs
      description: |
        Cursor-paginated list ordered by `created_at` descending. Filter
        parameters are additive; multiple query values AND together.
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: repo, in: query, schema: { type: string } }
        - { name: workflow_id, in: query, schema: { type: string } }
        - { name: state, in: query, schema: { type: string, enum: [pending, running, succeeded, failed, cancelled] } }
        - name: pull_request_url
          in: query
          description: |
            Equality filter on `run.pull_request_url` (#216). Used by
            the threaded-runs view to render every run on a PR.
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: trigger_ref
          in: query
          description: |
            Equality filter on `run.trigger_ref` (e.g. `issue:42`).
            Used by the threaded-runs view to render every run on the
            same trigger when no PR has landed yet.
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: runner_kind
          in: query
          description: |
            Equality filter on `run.runner_kind` (ADR-022 / #388).
            Used by compliance consumers to project to
            `github_actions` only (or to scope to local-runner
            dev-loop runs).
          schema: { type: string, enum: [github_actions, local] }
        - { name: limit, in: query, schema: { type: integer, default: 50, maximum: 200 } }
        - { name: cursor, in: query, schema: { type: string } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Page of runs.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                allOf:
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Pagination'
                  - type: object
                    properties:
                      items:
                        type: array
                        items: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Run' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '503': { $ref: '#/components/responses/ServiceUnavailable' }
    post:
      operationId: createRun
      summary: Create a workflow run
      description: |
        Creates a `runs` row in the `pending` state and dispatches a
        `workflow_dispatch` event to the customer's repo. The runner
        action picks it up and proceeds through the configured stages.

        Pass `Idempotency-Key: <opaque-string>` to make the call
        retriable. If a run with the same `(repo, key)` exists, it's
        returned with a 200 instead of creating a fresh one. The
        scope is `(repo, key)` so two callers picking the same key
        for different repos don't collide.
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - in: header
          name: Idempotency-Key
          required: false
          schema:
            type: string
            description: |
              Opaque caller-chosen string. Same key + same repo =
              the existing run is returned (200). Different key OR
              different repo = a fresh run is created (201). No
              header = always create.
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [repo, workflow_id, workflow_sha, trigger_source]
              properties:
                repo:
                  type: string
                  example: kuhlman-labs/fishhawk
                workflow_id:
                  type: string
                  example: feature_change
                workflow_sha:
                  type: string
                trigger_source:
                  type: string
                  enum: [github_issue, cli, ui]
                trigger_ref:
                  type: [string, "null"]
                runner_kind:
                  type: string
                  enum: [github_actions, local]
                  description: |
                    Optional. Execution backend the run will use
                    (ADR-022 / #388). Defaults to `github_actions`
                    when omitted. The local-runner CLI (Phase C of
                    E22 / #389) passes `local` to mint a run that
                    runs on the operator's workstation instead of
                    GitHub Actions.
                workflow_spec:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional. YAML body of `.fishhawk/workflows.yaml`
                    at `workflow_sha`, sent inline so the backend can
                    create one Stage row per stage definition for
                    API-minted runs (#411). When omitted AND the
                    backend has a GitHub client configured, the
                    handler resolves the App's installation for
                    `repo` and fetches the spec at `workflow_sha`
                    via the GitHub Contents API (#413) — used by
                    MCP-driven runs and cross-repo CLI flows that
                    can't easily ship the spec inline. When
                    omitted AND no GitHub client is configured,
                    the run row is created with no stages (the
                    legacy shape, kept for integration test
                    seeding). The `fishhawk run start` CLI always
                    sends this field, populated either via
                    auto-discovery of the local checkout or the
                    `--spec-file` flag. A malformed or
                    unrecognised-workflow_id body returns 400
                    before the run row is created; an App that's
                    not installed on the target repo returns 422
                    `repo_not_installed`; a missing spec at the
                    requested `workflow_sha` returns 422
                    `spec_not_found`.
                issue_context:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/IssueContext'
                  description: |
                    Optional. Cached payload from the operator's
                    `gh issue view` (#415). Carries the triggering
                    GitHub issue's title, body, url, and number so
                    the backend's prompt builder reads from the row
                    instead of needing an installation_id to fetch
                    from GitHub. Only valid with
                    `trigger_source: github_issue` — a 400 fires
                    when shipped with any other trigger source.
                    Webhook-dispatched runs leave this empty and
                    the backend falls back to the existing
                    GitHub-fetch path.
                drive:
                  type: boolean
                  description: |
                    Optional. Per-run drive-mode override (#1023 /
                    #996 theme 1). When present, wins over the
                    workflow spec's `drive` default; when omitted,
                    the run inherits the spec value (false when the
                    spec doesn't set one or no spec is resolved).
                    The resolved flag is snapshotted on the run row
                    at create time and echoed back as `drive` on the
                    Run resource.
                budget_override:
                  type: boolean
                  description: |
                    Optional. Operator force-past for a blocking
                    periodic cost budget (#688 / ADR-030). When the
                    resolved workflow declares an
                    `enforcement: blocking` budget whose current
                    calendar-period spend has reached `limit_usd`, a
                    new run is normally refused with `402
                    budget_exhausted`. Setting this `true` admits the
                    run anyway and records a
                    `run_admitted_budget_override` audit entry.
                    Ignored when no blocking budget is over — it only
                    matters at the moment a budget would block. There
                    is no override on the webhook-dispatch path
                    (webhook triggers can't carry one).
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Idempotent replay — existing run returned unchanged.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Run' }
        '201':
          description: Run created and dispatched.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Run' }
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '402':
          description: |
            `budget_exhausted` — the resolved workflow declares an
            `enforcement: blocking` periodic cost budget whose
            current calendar-period spend has reached `limit_usd`
            (#688 / ADR-030). No run row is created. Clear it by
            raising `limit_usd`, switching the budget to
            `enforcement: advisory`, or retrying with
            `budget_override: true`. In-flight runs and CI-retry /
            decomposition-child continuations are never gated.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '403': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden' }
        '422':
          description: |
            Returned when the GitHub-fetch fallback can't resolve
            the spec (#413). `repo_not_installed` when the App is
            absent from the target repo; `spec_not_found` when the
            file doesn't exist at the requested `workflow_sha`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503': { $ref: '#/components/responses/ServiceUnavailable' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}:
    get:
      operationId: getRun
      summary: Get a run
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Run details.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Run' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/cancel:
    post:
      operationId: cancelRun
      summary: Cancel a run
      description: |
        Transitions the run and any non-terminal stages to `cancelled`.
        Idempotent — calling on an already-cancelled or already-terminal
        run returns 409 unless `state` already equals `cancelled`.
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Run cancelled.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Run' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '409': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Conflict' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/consolidate:
    post:
      operationId: consolidateRun
      summary: Run the decomposed-parent fan-in (E24.2) on demand
      description: |
        Operator-drivable, error-SURFACING fan-in trigger (E24.2 / ADR-041 /
        #1238) for a decomposed PARENT run parked in `awaiting_children`. The
        60s child-completion sweeper is the automatic backstop that normally
        runs the fan-in, but it is OFF by default in the local dev `fishhawkd`
        ("dev-loop posture"), so on the local runner a parent whose children
        have all settled stays parked with no consolidated branch/PR. This
        endpoint runs the same fan-in on demand AND surfaces why it failed:
        where the event-driven path WARN-swallows a non-conflict
        `IntegrateSlices` error (leaving a silent stuck parent), this returns
        it (502 `slice_integration_error`).

        Mechanism: composes the existing orchestrator primitives
        (`IntegrateSlices` → `TransitionStage` → `Advance`), mirroring the
        sweeper's `resolveParent`, WITHOUT touching the hot event-driven /
        sweeper paths. On a clean integration the parent implement stage
        resolves `succeeded`, a `children_settled` audit entry is appended
        (payload byte-identical to the sweeper's), the consolidated PR opens,
        and the parent review gate dispatches. On a slice merge conflict the
        parent implement stage fails recoverable (category `B`) and a
        `slice_integration_conflict` audit entry records the conflicting slice
        — the E24.2 contract, preserved.

        **Preconditions** (each a refusal): the run is a decomposed parent
        (`decomposed_from` null AND it has ≥1 decomposed child, else 400
        `not_a_decomposed_parent`); its implement stage is parked in
        `awaiting_children` (else 409 `not_awaiting_children` — already
        resolved or not a decomposition); EVERY child is terminal (else 409
        `children_in_flight`) AND every terminal child `succeeded` (else 409
        `children_failed` — resolve/re-drive the failed child rather than
        consolidating a partial set).

        Idempotent-friendly: a second call after a successful consolidate (or
        after the sweeper won the race) finds no `awaiting_children` stage and
        returns 409 `not_awaiting_children`; `IntegrateSlices`' own merges are
        idempotent no-ops, so a retry after a surfaced error re-enters cleanly.

        **Authorization.** Requires `write:runs`. A run-bound MCP token
        (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) is refused 403 (`agent_token_forbidden`) —
        consolidation is an operator action, not one the implement agent
        self-drives.
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            Fan-in ran. `outcome` is `integrated` (every slice merged; parent
            implement `succeeded`; `consolidated_branch` + `pull_request_url`
            set) or `slice_conflict` (a slice failed to merge; parent implement
            failed recoverable category `B`; `conflicting_slice_index` +
            `conflicting_child_run_id` set).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  run_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
                  outcome:
                    type: string
                    enum: [integrated, slice_conflict]
                    description: 'integrated = clean fan-in; slice_conflict = a slice failed to merge (parent failed recoverable).'
                  resolved_to_state:
                    type: string
                    description: 'The parent implement stage state after the fan-in: succeeded (integrated) or failed (slice_conflict).'
                  consolidated_branch: { type: string, description: 'The consolidated branch name; set on the integrated outcome.' }
                  pull_request_url: { type: string, description: 'The consolidated PR URL; set on the integrated outcome.' }
                  conflicting_slice_index: { type: integer, description: 'The slice index that failed to merge; set on the slice_conflict outcome.' }
                  conflicting_child_run_id: { type: string, description: 'The child run id owning the conflicting slice; set on the slice_conflict outcome.' }
                  detail: { type: string, description: 'Human-readable conflict detail; set on the slice_conflict outcome.' }
        '400':
          description: |
            The run is not a decomposed parent (a child, or has no children).
            Body's `error` is `not_a_decomposed_parent`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            One of two cases: (1) bearer token lacks `write:runs` scope — body
            `error` is `insufficient_scope`; (2) a run-bound MCP token attempted
            the fan-in — body `error` is `agent_token_forbidden`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '409':
          description: |
            Fan-in not runnable. One of: `not_awaiting_children` (no implement
            stage parked in awaiting_children), `children_in_flight` (a child is
            still non-terminal; `details.non_terminal_child_run_ids` names
            them), or `children_failed` (a child failed;
            `details.failed_child_run_ids` names them).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '502':
          description: |
            The slice integration failed with a non-conflict error (e.g. a base
            ref or branch-create failure). Body's `error` is
            `slice_integration_error` and `details.error` carries the cause. The
            parent implement stage is left `awaiting_children` for retry.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/integrate-wave:
    post:
      operationId: integrateWave
      summary: Non-settling per-wave fan-in of a decomposed parent (E24.X / #1278)
      description: |
        The NON-settling per-wave fan-in (#1278 slice B / ADR-041) the
        topological-wave `fishhawk_run_children` dispatch calls BETWEEN waves.
        It merges the slices SUCCEEDED SO FAR onto the consolidated branch so a
        later wave's dependent slices cut their branch from a tree that already
        contains the predecessors' merged symbols.

        Mechanism: reuses the EXACT same exported `IntegrateSlices` primitive
        `POST /consolidate` uses — no new git-merge code — which filters to
        succeeded slices ascending and is idempotent (already-merged 204 /
        branch-exists 422 no-ops).

        **How it DIFFERS from `/consolidate` (the terminal fan-in).** Unlike
        `/consolidate`, integrate-wave is intermediate and deliberately does
        NOT settle the parent:
        - it does NOT require all children terminal (it merges whatever has
          succeeded so far);
        - it does NOT transition the parent implement stage (the stage state is
          identical before and after — on BOTH the integrated and the
          slice_conflict outcome; the driver decides what to do on a conflict);
        - it does NOT advance the run or open the consolidated PR.
        The final terminal fan-in (stage resolution + consolidated PR) stays
        driven by `/consolidate` after the LAST wave's children settle.

        **Preconditions** (each a refusal): the run is a decomposed parent
        (`decomposed_from` null AND it has ≥1 decomposed child, else 400
        `not_a_decomposed_parent`). There is intentionally NO
        terminal-children / awaiting_children precondition — that is the whole
        point of the per-wave fan-in.

        **Authorization.** Requires `write:runs`. A run-bound MCP token
        (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) is refused 403 (`agent_token_forbidden`) —
        the fan-in is an operator action.
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            The per-wave fan-in ran. `outcome` is `integrated` (the succeeded
            slices so far merged; `consolidated_branch` set) or `slice_conflict`
            (a slice failed to merge; `conflicting_slice_index` +
            `conflicting_child_run_id` set). The parent stage is UNCHANGED on
            both outcomes — there is no `resolved_to_state`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  run_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
                  outcome:
                    type: string
                    enum: [integrated, slice_conflict]
                    description: 'integrated = the succeeded slices merged onto the consolidated branch; slice_conflict = a slice failed to merge (parent stage left unchanged).'
                  consolidated_branch: { type: string, description: 'The consolidated branch name; set on the integrated outcome.' }
                  conflicting_slice_index: { type: integer, description: 'The slice index that failed to merge; set on the slice_conflict outcome.' }
                  conflicting_child_run_id: { type: string, description: 'The child run id owning the conflicting slice; set on the slice_conflict outcome.' }
                  detail: { type: string, description: 'Human-readable conflict detail; set on the slice_conflict outcome.' }
        '400':
          description: |
            The run is not a decomposed parent (a child, or has no children).
            Body's `error` is `not_a_decomposed_parent`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            One of two cases: (1) bearer token lacks `write:runs` scope — body
            `error` is `insufficient_scope`; (2) a run-bound MCP token attempted
            the fan-in — body `error` is `agent_token_forbidden`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '502':
          description: |
            The slice integration failed with a non-conflict error (e.g. a base
            ref or branch-create failure). Body's `error` is
            `slice_integration_error` and `details.error` carries the cause. The
            parent stage is left unchanged for retry.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/recover:
    post:
      operationId: recoverRun
      summary: Recover a category-B-failed run against its approved plan
      description: |
        Operator-initiated category-B recovery (#978). The target shape
        — top-level run vs. decomposition child — is auto-detected from
        the run's `decomposed_from`:

        **Top-level target.** Mints a NEW plan-stage-less child run that
        re-executes against the parent run's approved plan — the same
        inheritance shape as the CI-failure auto-retry, but
        operator-triggered and exempt from the `on_ci_failure` cap: the
        child carries the parent's `retry_attempt` UNCHANGED, with
        `parent_run_id` as provenance. Responds 201 with the new run.

        **Decomposition-child target (#1081).** When the target run is
        itself a decomposition child (`decomposed_from` set), recovery
        re-drives THAT child IN PLACE — the same run id, NOT a freshly
        minted run — on the shared parent branch, so the parent fan-out
        parked in `awaiting_children` can still consolidate. An in-place
        re-drive (rather than a new child sharing `decomposed_from`) is
        deliberate: a second child row would double-count in the
        parent's consolidation counters. The child's failed implement
        stage is re-opened (`failed` → `pending`) and the run
        un-terminal-ed (`failed` → `running`), then handed to the
        orchestrator to dispatch; its plan resolves via the existing
        `parent_run_id` walk. Responds 201 with the re-opened child.

        **Eligibility.** A top-level target requires the parent's plan
        stage `succeeded` AND its implement stage `failed` category `B`
        (scope/constraint violation). A decomposition-child target
        requires the CHILD's own implement stage `failed` category `B`
        AND a plan resolvable via the `parent_run_id` walk. Anything
        else is refused 409 `recovery_not_eligible` with `details`
        naming `{plan_state, implement_state, failure_category,
        plan_resolved}`. The stage-level retry endpoint keeps refusing
        category B — recovery is broader than a retry, not a retry of
        the terminal stage.

        **Scope amendments.** Optional `add_scope_files` paths land as
        a PRE-APPROVED #961 scope-amendment row on the implement stage
        — the newly minted one for a top-level target, the EXISTING
        re-opened one for a decomposition child (its stage id is
        preserved across the re-drive, so the fold by run + stage id
        still sees it) (visible via the scope-amendments list, folded
        by the prompt fetch and the runner's pre-commit refresh).
        `operation: create` entries flow into the net-new-file gates
        like any approved amendment. Note the fold deliberately no-ops
        when the reused plan declares an empty `scope.files` (the
        `git add -A` guard) — category-B failures presuppose a
        declared scope, so this is a documented edge, not a refusal.

        **Scope exemptions.** Optional `exempt_scope_files` is the
        inverse of `add_scope_files` (#1229): each `{path, reason}` marks
        a DECLARED `scope.files` path operator-justified-unchanged so the
        runner's `MissingScopeFiles` shortfall gate subtracts it — a
        category-B run that failed SOLELY on a declared-but-unchanged file
        recovers in one call with no replan. Unlike `add_scope_files`, an
        exemption does NOT mint a scope-amendment row (it subtracts from
        the gate, it does not widen scope). Each path must be clean
        repo-relative AND carry a non-empty reason; either failure is
        refused 400 `validation_failed` with `field=exempt_scope_files`.
        Exemptions are recorded as `exempted_paths` on the
        `plan_reused_from` provenance and delivered to the runner via the
        implement prompt's `scope_exemptions` field.

        **Provenance.** A `plan_reused_from` audit entry is appended
        on the child with
        `{parent_run_id, parent_failure_category, added_paths,
        exempted_paths, source, reason}`; `source` is `operator_recovery`
        for a top-level target and `decomposition_child_recovery` for an
        in-place child re-drive. The approved amendment row is the second
        surface. The `reason` is additionally injected into the recovery
        agent's binding conditions (Part D, #1229).

        No `workflow_dispatch` is fired — identical to `POST /v0/runs`:
        local-runner runs sit pending for the local loop to drive. The
        implement stage's prompt resolves the parent's plan via the
        existing `parent_run_id` walk; the parent's binding approval
        conditions and approval-time `add_scope_files` are inherited.

        `Idempotency-Key` shares the `(repo, key)` keyspace with
        `POST /v0/runs`: a replay returns the existing run with 200.
        Replay is honored BEFORE the budget gate — a network retry of
        a successful recovery returns the existing run even when the
        blocking budget tripped between the two calls (replay is not
        new spend).

        **Authorization.** Requires `write:runs` (same as
        `POST /v0/runs`). A decomposition-child target ADDITIONALLY
        rejects agent (MCP subject-bound, `mcp:run:<uuid>`) tokens with
        403 `agent_token_forbidden`: that path re-opens a terminal run
        via the same `run.RedriveChild` action `POST /v0/runs/{id}/redrive`
        performs, which is operator-only — an agent may not re-drive any
        run, including its own (#698, #1081).
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - in: header
          name: Idempotency-Key
          required: false
          schema:
            type: string
            description: |
              Opaque caller-chosen string, shared keyspace with
              `POST /v0/runs`. Same key + same repo = the existing
              run is returned (200).
      requestBody:
        required: false
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              additionalProperties: false
              properties:
                add_scope_files:
                  type: array
                  description: |
                    Operator-named paths folded into the recovery run's
                    effective scope as a pre-approved scope amendment.
                    Repo-relative; absolute, empty, and `..`-containing
                    paths are refused 400.
                  items:
                    type: object
                    additionalProperties: false
                    required: [path]
                    properties:
                      path:
                        type: string
                        example: docs/companion.md
                      operation:
                        type: string
                        enum: [modify, create]
                        default: modify
                exempt_scope_files:
                  type: array
                  description: |
                    DECLARED `scope.files` paths to mark
                    operator-justified-unchanged so the runner's shortfall
                    gate subtracts them (#1229) — the inverse of
                    `add_scope_files`: it subtracts from the gate, it does
                    NOT widen scope (no amendment row). Each entry requires
                    a clean repo-relative `path` AND a non-empty `reason`;
                    either failure is refused 400 `validation_failed` with
                    `field=exempt_scope_files`.
                  items:
                    type: object
                    additionalProperties: false
                    required: [path, reason]
                    properties:
                      path:
                        type: string
                        example: backend/internal/server/handlers.go
                      reason:
                        type: string
                        example: the declared file needs no change on this slice
                reason:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Why the recovery (and each added path) is needed;
                    recorded on the amendment row and the
                    `plan_reused_from` audit entry, and injected into the
                    recovery agent's binding conditions (Part D).
                budget_override:
                  type: boolean
                  description: |
                    Force the recovery past a blocking periodic budget
                    that is over its limit (#688); ignored when no
                    blocking budget is over.
      responses:
        '201':
          description: |
            Recovery run created (top-level target) or decomposition
            child re-opened in place (same run id).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Run' }
        '200':
          description: Idempotent replay — existing run returned.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Run' }
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            Missing `write:runs` scope (`insufficient_scope`), OR a
            decomposition-child target presented with an MCP/agent
            subject-bound token (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) — body `error`
            is `agent_token_forbidden`; that path re-drives a terminal run
            in place, an operator-only action (#698, #1081).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '409':
          description: |
            `recovery_not_eligible` — a top-level target whose plan
            stage is not `succeeded` and/or whose implement stage is not
            `failed` category-B, or a decomposition-child target whose
            own implement stage is not `failed` category-B and/or whose
            plan does not resolve via the `parent_run_id` walk.
            `details` carries `{plan_state, implement_state,
            failure_category, plan_resolved}`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '422':
          description: |
            `recovery_unsupported` — the parent run has no cached
            workflow spec (legacy row), its cached spec no longer
            parses or defines the workflow, or the workflow has no
            non-plan stages. Start a fresh run instead.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503': { $ref: '#/components/responses/ServiceUnavailable' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/redrive:
    post:
      operationId: redriveChild
      summary: Re-drive a failed decomposition child run
      description: |
        Operator recovery action for a decomposition parent parked in
        `awaiting_children` because every failed child was in a retryable
        failure category (A/C, or D-timeout) — see #698. Re-opens the
        failed child run (`failed` → `running`) and its failed implement
        stage (`failed` → `pending`), then hands off to the orchestrator
        to walk `pending` → `dispatched` and fire `workflow_dispatch`.
        Un-terminal-ing the run is what lets the orchestrator act —
        `Advance` no-ops on a terminal run. On the child's next terminal
        transition the parked parent reconciles through the normal
        parent-resolution path. Writes a `child_redriven` audit entry on
        success with the prior implement-stage failure category and
        reason in the payload.

        **Authorization.** Re-drive is an operator action. It requires
        the operator retry scope (`write:stages` or `write:retries`, the
        same shape as `POST /v0/stages/{stage_id}/retry`) AND rejects any
        MCP subject-bound (agent) token outright — an agent may not
        re-drive any run (not its own, not a sibling/child/parent),
        because re-opening a terminal run is not an agent-permitted
        action. Cookie-session operators are accepted unconditionally.
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Child re-driven; refreshed Run row (state `running`) in body.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Run' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            One of two cases: (1) the caller presented an MCP/agent
            subject-bound token (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) — body `error`
            is `agent_token_forbidden`; agent tokens may never re-drive a
            run. (2) a bearer token lacks both `write:stages` and
            `write:retries` scope — body `error` is `insufficient_scope`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '409': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Conflict' }
        '422':
          description: |
            Re-drive not applicable: the run is not a decomposition child
            (`DecomposedFrom` is null), is not in the `failed` state, or
            has no failed implement stage to re-open. Body's `error` is
            `redrive_not_applicable` and `message` explains the reason.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/reset-branch:
    post:
      operationId: resetRunBranch
      summary: Force-reset a run branch off a foreign on-top commit
      description: |
        Operator-gated, audited ADR-035 remediation (#867) for a foreign
        commit pushed **ON TOP** of a run's own commits on the open PR
        branch — the post-prevention (#861/#865) residual vector. Rewinds
        the run/PR branch back to its **last run-authored HEAD** (the newest
        commit attributable to this run's reported-head ledger), dropping
        the on-top foreign commit, then re-parks the review gate so CI + the
        merge reconciler re-evaluate the rewound head.

        Mechanism: resolves the PR base ref + live head from GitHub, builds
        the run's reported-head ledger (seeded `""` so the foreign tip is
        not self-whitelisted), runs the `base...head` compare, and computes
        the last run-authored HEAD. It then force-updates (`PATCH .../git/
        refs/heads/{branch}` with `force:true`) the PR head ref to that SHA,
        re-parks the run's review stage, writes a `branch_reset` audit entry,
        and refreshes the sticky status comment.

        **Destructive — safety invariants (all BINDING):**

        - **Operator-gated.** `confirm` MUST be `true`; a missing/false
          confirm is refused 400 (`confirmation_required`). Never silent/auto.
        - **On-top only.** Proceeds only when EVERY foreign commit sits
          strictly above the reset target. A foreign ancestor/interleaved
          commit cannot be dropped by a reset (the run's commits sit on it)
          and is refused 422 (`reset_out_of_scope`) — prevention owns it.
        - **Fail-closed.** Unlike the detect path (which fails open so a
          transient GitHub failure never blocks a clean run), the
          destructive action REFUSES on ANY uncertainty — unresolvable base
          ref, incomplete ledger, compare error, no identifiable
          run-authored HEAD — with 422 (`reset_not_determinable`), never a
          force-update.
        - **Lease re-check.** Immediately before the force-update the live
          PR head is re-read; if it changed since classification (concurrent
          push) the reset aborts 422 (`reset_not_determinable`). The REST
          refs API has no compare-and-swap, so this narrows but does not
          eliminate the TOCTOU window.

        The dropped commit stays recoverable from the remote reflog / the
        foreign pusher's own branch (recorded in the audit `recovery_note`).

        **Authorization.** Requires `write:runs`. A run-bound MCP token
        (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) may reset ONLY its own run's branch;
        another run's branch is refused 403 (`cross_run_reset`).
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        description: |
          Confirms the destructive force-rewind and carries an optional
          operator rationale.
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              additionalProperties: false
              required: [confirm]
              properties:
                confirm:
                  type: boolean
                  description: |
                    MUST be `true` to proceed — the reset force-rewinds the
                    PR head ref and is destructive. A missing/false value is
                    refused 400 (`confirmation_required`).
                reason:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional operator rationale, recorded on the
                    `branch_reset` audit entry.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Branch rewound; rewind summary in body.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  run_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
                  pr_number: { type: integer }
                  branch: { type: string }
                  dropped_offending_sha: { type: string, description: 'The foreign on-top commit that was dropped.' }
                  reset_to_sha: { type: string, description: 'The last run-authored HEAD the branch was rewound to.' }
                  prior_head_sha: { type: string, description: 'The branch tip before the rewind.' }
                  reparked_review_stage_id: { type: string, description: 'The re-parked review stage id; omitted on the commit-yourself shape.' }
                  recovery_note: { type: string }
        '400':
          description: |
            Malformed body or `confirm` not `true`. Body's `error` is
            `validation_failed` or `confirmation_required`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            One of two cases: (1) bearer token lacks `write:runs` scope —
            body `error` is `insufficient_scope`; (2) an mcp run-bound token
            (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) targeting another run's branch — body
            `error` is `cross_run_reset`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '422':
          description: |
            Reset refused. One of: `reset_out_of_scope` (the foreign commit
            is an ancestor/interleaved, not strictly on top — prevention
            owns it); `reset_not_applicable` (the tip is already the last
            run-authored HEAD; nothing on top to drop); or
            `reset_not_determinable` (fail-closed: the lineage could not be
            classified with certainty, or the lease re-check found a
            concurrent push). Body's `message` explains the specific reason.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '502':
          description: |
            The GitHub force-update of the PR head ref failed
            (`reset_force_update_failed`).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/vouch-commit:
    post:
      operationId: vouchCommit
      summary: Vouch a foreign commit as run-authored lineage
      description: |
        Operator-gated, audited ADR-035 provenance path (#1044) for a
        foreign commit on a run branch that no loop-native remediation can
        route — an operator's mechanical remediation commit (e.g. a
        `scripts/sync-schemas` output pushed onto a decomposition fan-out
        branch whose children are all terminal with zero open concerns). The
        operator DECLARES the commit run-authored lineage; the declaration
        records an `operator_commit_vouched` audit entry and unions the
        vouched SHA into the run's reported-head ledger (on the run's own
        chain AND its decomposition children), so the merge reconciler's
        ADR-035 re-check (`ReverifyBranchLineage`) attributes it cleanly and
        the run it fixed is no longer wedged.

        Distinct from `reset-branch`, which DROPS an on-top foreign commit:
        vouch KEEPS the operator commit and attributes it. Use vouch when
        the commit is a legitimate remediation to retain.

        **Fail-closed is preserved.** The handler records the declaration
        verbatim — it does NOT verify the SHA exists on the branch or in the
        compare set. Vouching a wrong/non-existent SHA simply adds an
        unreachable ledger entry and un-wedges nothing; an UN-vouched foreign
        commit still fails category-B at the report boundary and still blocks
        merge resolution.

        **Authorization.** Operator-token-only by design (ADR-035 sole-writer
        invariant). Requires `write:stages`. A run-bound MCP token (subject
        `mcp:run:<uuid>`) is REJECTED OUTRIGHT 403 (`run_token_forbidden`),
        even for its own run — an agent self-declaring lineage for a commit
        on its own branch would defeat the invariant the vouch must preserve.
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        description: |
          Names the commit to vouch and carries the operator's rationale.
          Both fields are required — the vouch is an audited operator
          declaration.
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              additionalProperties: false
              required: [sha, reason]
              properties:
                sha:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    The commit SHA on the run branch to declare as
                    run-authored lineage.
                reason:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Operator rationale — why this commit is legitimate
                    run-authored lineage. Recorded verbatim on the
                    `operator_commit_vouched` audit entry.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Vouch recorded; the declaration is echoed in the body.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  run_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
                  vouched_sha: { type: string, description: 'The commit SHA recorded as run-authored lineage.' }
                  reason: { type: string }
        '400':
          description: |
            Malformed body or an empty `sha`/`reason`. Body's `error` is
            `validation_failed`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            One of two cases: (1) the identity lacks `write:stages` scope —
            body `error` is `insufficient_scope` (enforced unconditionally:
            a cookie-session operator without the scope is rejected too, no
            bypass); (2) a run-bound MCP token
            (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) attempted the vouch — body `error` is
            `run_token_forbidden` (rejected outright, even for its own run).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '503':
          description: |
            The run or audit repositories are not wired
            (`vouch_unconfigured`).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/stages:
    get:
      operationId: listRunStages
      summary: List stages for a run
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Stages ordered by sequence ascending.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                required: [items]
                properties:
                  items:
                    type: array
                    items: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Stage' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/stages/{stage_id}:
    get:
      operationId: getRunStage
      summary: Read one stage by its (run, stage) handle, with optional terminal-wait long-poll
      description: |
        Resolves a stage by the durable ADR-037 `(run_id, stage_id)`
        handle and returns the canonical `Stage` shape plus a small
        wait envelope (`state`, `terminal`, optional `next_action`). It
        is the SDK-independent REST analogue of the scope-amendment
        `?wait` long-poll (#1252), giving a detached operator-side
        watcher (a backgrounded shell poll) ONE authoritative signal
        for stage settledness. Companion to the `dispatch_stage`
        non-blocking dispatch work (#1232).

        **Auth** mirrors `GET /v0/runs/{run_id}/scope-amendments`:
        anonymous callers get 401; a run-bound `fhm_` bearer must carry
        `mcp:read` (else 403 `insufficient_scope`) AND match the path
        `run_id` (else 403 `cross_run_stage`); operator bearers/sessions
        (not run-bound) pass. The `(run_id, stage_id)` handle must be
        consistent — a stage whose `run_id` differs from the path is
        404 `stage_not_found` (it does not exist at this path).

        **`terminal`** is true when the stage has *settled*: a terminal
        state (`succeeded`, `failed`, `cancelled`) OR a parked state
        awaiting operator action (`awaiting_approval`,
        `awaiting_children`, `awaiting_input`, `awaiting_scope_decision`).
        The in-flight states (`pending`, `dispatched`, `running`) are
        not settled. This is wider than run/stage terminality — a
        detached watcher releases the moment the stage needs attention.

        **Optional `?wait=<seconds>` long-poll.** When `wait` is a
        positive integer and the stage is not yet settled, the server
        holds the connection — re-reading the stage internally — and
        returns the moment the stage settles, at the wait cap (returning
        the still-unsettled stage so the client re-issues), or on client
        disconnect. `wait` is clamped to `0..30`; `0`, absent, or a
        non-positive/non-integer value is the unchanged immediate read.
        An already-settled stage returns immediately regardless of
        `wait`. On a transient re-read error the server returns the
        last-good stage at 200 (never regressing the wait to a 500); no
        new error codes.

        **`next_action`** reuses the same `run_auto_advanced` drive
        distillation as `GET /v0/runs/{run_id}`: best-effort, omitted on
        non-drive runs, terminal runs, and any read failure — never
        fabricated.
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - { name: stage_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - name: wait
          in: query
          required: false
          description: |
            Optional server-side long-poll in seconds (#1252). `0` or
            absent = no wait (immediate read). When positive and the
            stage is not yet settled, holds until the stage settles
            (terminal OR parked) or the wait elapses; the client
            re-issues to extend. Clamped to `0..30`.
          schema:
            type: integer
            minimum: 0
            maximum: 30
            default: 0
      responses:
        '200':
          description: The stage, with the terminal-wait envelope.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                allOf:
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Stage'
                  - type: object
                    required: [state, terminal]
                    properties:
                      state:
                        type: string
                        description: The stage state, echoed at the top level.
                      terminal:
                        type: boolean
                        description: |
                          True when the stage has settled — terminal OR
                          parked awaiting operator action.
                      next_action:
                        $ref: '#/components/schemas/RunNextAction'
                        description: |
                          Distilled operator next step, reusing the
                          `GET /v0/runs/{run_id}` drive distillation.
                          Omitted on non-drive runs, terminal runs, and
                          on any read failure.
        '400':
          description: run_id or stage_id is malformed (`validation_failed`).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401':
          description: No authenticated token.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '403':
          description: |
            `insufficient_scope` (run-bound token lacks `mcp:read`)
            or `cross_run_stage` (run-bound token reading another run's
            stage).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404':
          description: |
            `stage_not_found` — no stage with that id, or the stage's
            `run_id` does not match the path `run_id` (inconsistent
            handle).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503':
          description: Run repository not wired.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/audit:
    get:
      operationId: listRunAudit
      summary: List audit entries for a run
      description: |
        Cursor-paginated, sequence-ascending. Used by the run-detail UI
        and (eventually) the compliance export.
      tags: [audit]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - { name: category, in: query, schema: { type: string } }
        - name: stage_id
          in: query
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
          description: |
            Optional. When set, the response is filtered to entries
            tagged with this stage id. Used by the implement-stage
            session view (#215) to render a per-stage activity feed.
        - name: since_sequence
          in: query
          schema: { type: integer, format: int64, minimum: 0 }
          description: |
            Optional. When set, the response is filtered to entries with
            `sequence` strictly greater than this value (applied before
            pagination). `0` is a no-op (sequences start at 1). This is
            the anchor for the sequence-anchored await primitive
            (`fishhawk_await_audit`, #962): a caller polling for "the
            next `<category>` entry after sequence N" never needs
            pagination or a tail-sized limit.
        - { name: limit, in: query, schema: { type: integer, default: 100, maximum: 500 } }
        - { name: cursor, in: query, schema: { type: string } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Page of audit entries.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                allOf:
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Pagination'
                  - type: object
                    properties:
                      items:
                        type: array
                        items: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/AuditEntry' }
        '400':
          description: |
            `validation_failed` — `stage_id` not a UUID, `since_sequence`
            not a non-negative integer, or `limit` out of range.
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/budget:
    get:
      operationId: getRunBudget
      summary: Current periodic-budget status for a run's workflow
      description: |
        Returns the current calendar-period status of the workflow's
        periodic budget (#693 / ADR-030): spend vs limit, the fraction
        consumed, and the `ok`/`warn`/`over` tier. Reuses the same
        evaluation chain the advisory alert path runs (period range →
        cross-run cost sum → evaluate).

        **Display-only.** This endpoint never gates or blocks a run; it
        exists so the MCP loop can surface spend-vs-limit every stage
        instead of relying on the advisory issue comment.

        When the workflow declares no budget the response is **200 with
        an empty object** (`{}`) rather than a 404, so clients branch on
        the presence of `period`. A workflow with multiple budgets
        surfaces only the first.
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            Periodic-budget status, or an empty object when the workflow
            declares no budget.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/RunBudgetStatus' }
        '400':
          description: "`validation_failed` — `run_id` not a UUID."
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '503': { description: Run repository unavailable. }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/cache-efficiency:
    get:
      operationId: getRunCacheEfficiency
      summary: Per-run prompt-cache efficiency metric
      description: |
        Returns the run's prompt-cache efficiency, derived purely from the
        run's `cost_recorded` audit ledger (ADR-044 / #1352): the cache-read
        ratio (share of input served from cache), the reuse factor (re-reads
        per cache-write token), and the gross / penalty / net US-dollar
        effect of caching, plus a per-stage breakdown keyed on the entry's
        `source` (`plan_review` / `implement_review`, with the runner
        stage-agent path — entries that carry no `source` — bucketed as
        `agent`).

        **Display-only.** This endpoint never gates or blocks a run; it
        exists so the MCP loop can surface and maximize cache-hit usage. It
        has no write path and no migration — it reads existing audit data.

        When the run has no `cost_recorded` data the response is **200 with
        an empty object** (`{}`) rather than a 404, so clients branch on the
        presence of a field. An unknown / unpriced model contributes 0 to
        the savings figures while its tokens still count toward the ratios.
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            Cache-efficiency metric, or an empty object when the run has no
            cost data.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/RunCacheEfficiency' }
        '400':
          description: "`validation_failed` — `run_id` not a UUID."
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '503': { description: Run repository unavailable. }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/cost:
    get:
      operationId: getRunCost
      summary: Per-run estimated cost with per-stage and per-merged-PR rollup
      description: |
        Returns the run's estimated cost, derived purely from the run's
        `cost_recorded` audit ledger (#1372): the run's total estimated
        US-dollar cost, a per-stage breakdown keyed on the entry's `source`
        (`plan_review` / `implement_review`, with the runner stage-agent
        path — entries that carry no `source` — bucketed as `agent`), and —
        when the run resolved to a merged PR (a `pr_merged` audit row exists
        AND the run carries a pull-request URL) — a cost-per-merged-PR
        rollup summing `cost_usd_total` across every run sharing that PR URL
        plus the contributing run count.

        **Display-only.** This endpoint never gates or blocks a run; it
        exists so the MCP loop can surface the cost to land work. It has no
        write path and no migration — it reads existing audit and run data;
        merge state is read from the audit ledger, never the GitHub API.

        When the run has no `cost_recorded` data the response is **200 with
        an empty object** (`{}`) rather than a 404, so clients branch on the
        presence of a field.
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            Cost summary, or an empty object when the run has no cost data.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/RunCost' }
        '400':
          description: "`validation_failed` — `run_id` not a UUID."
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '503': { description: Run repository unavailable. }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/diagnostics:
    get:
      operationId: getRunDiagnostics
      summary: Product-facts-only diagnostic bundle for a run
      description: |
        Returns a redaction-safe diagnostic bundle for the run (#1006):
        the minimal, structured product facts an operator attaches to an
        upstream Fishhawk product report. The bundle carries the run id,
        ordered stage states, the failing stage's category + audit
        surface, the audit sequence range, this backend's build version +
        git SHA (and the minimum runner version it requires), the workflow
        spec hash, and the runner kind.

        **Pure read, no egress.** By construction the bundle carries NO
        diffs, paths, prompts, free text, or audit payload bodies — the
        failing stage's free-text reason is excluded. Backs the
        `fishhawk diagnose <run-id>` CLI verb and the foundation of the
        product-feedback egress path (later slices).
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: The product-facts diagnostic bundle.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/DiagnosticBundle' }
        '400':
          description: "`validation_failed` — `run_id` not a UUID."
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '503': { description: Run or audit repository unavailable. }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/product-reports:
    post:
      operationId: fileProductReport
      summary: File a deduped, audited upstream product report for a run
      description: |
        The deduped, audited egress path for upstream Fishhawk product
        feedback (#1006). Collects the run's product-facts diagnostic
        bundle, fingerprints the failure over (failure category, audit
        surface, fishhawkd version family), searches the FIXED upstream
        product repo for an open report already carrying that fingerprint,
        and either appends an occurrence comment (dedup hit, nothing
        created) or files a new fingerprint-marked report (dedup miss).
        A source-side `product_report_filed` audit entry names what left
        the boundary (fingerprint, destination, created-vs-occurrence).

        **Egress is gated** by a mutually-exclusive entitlement switch
        (widened in #1274). The report may be filed by the run's own
        run-bound agent token, a non-run-bound operator/operator-agent
        bearer token holding `write:runs`, or a cookie-session operator.
        A run-bound token bound to a DIFFERENT run → 403 `run_not_entitled`;
        a non-run-bound bearer WITHOUT `write:runs` → 403
        `insufficient_scope`. An operator/operator-agent token may file for
        ANY run (operator scope = the deployment). A per-repo
        `product_feedback` kill-switch (`enabled: false`) → 403
        `product_feedback_disabled`, files nothing.
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: false
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProductReportRequest' }
      responses:
        '201':
