          description: The report was filed (created) or an occurrence was recorded.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProductReportResponse' }
        '400':
          description: "`validation_failed` — `run_id` not a UUID or `kind` not bug/feature."
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            `run_not_entitled` — a run-bound token may only file for its own
            run; `insufficient_scope` — a non-run-bound operator bearer is
            missing `write:runs`; `product_feedback_disabled` — the repo's
            kill-switch is off.
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '501':
          description: "`provider_unimplemented` — no feedback provider registered for the conventions' provider."
        '502':
          description: "`product_report_failed` — the dedup search or upstream filing failed."
        '503': { description: Run or audit repository unavailable. }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/status-comment:
    get:
      operationId: getStatusComment
      summary: Get rendered sticky-comment body and stored GitHub comment ID
      description: |
        Returns the rendered Markdown body for the run's issue thread sticky
        comment (`RenderStatusBody`) plus the `github_comment_id` from the
        most recent `status_comment_posted` audit entry (0 when no comment
        has been posted yet). Used by the CLI to drive edit-in-place updates
        without re-rendering client-side (#428).
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Rendered body and stored comment ID.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/StatusCommentResponse' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '503': { description: Run repository unavailable. }
    post:
      operationId: recordStatusCommentID
      summary: Record the GitHub comment ID after posting the sticky comment
      description: |
        Called by the CLI after `gh api` creates or edits the issue comment.
        Appends a `status_comment_posted` audit entry with the returned
        `github_comment_id` so subsequent GET calls can return it for
        edit-in-place targeting. Idempotent: multiple POSTs are safe (#428).
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [github_comment_id]
              properties:
                github_comment_id:
                  type: integer
                  format: int64
                  minimum: 1
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Comment ID recorded.
        '400':
          description: "`validation_failed` — `github_comment_id` missing or ≤ 0."
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '503': { description: Run repository unavailable. }

  /v0/audit:
    get:
      operationId: listGlobalAudit
      summary: Search the cross-chain audit log
      description: |
        Cross-chain feed for the audit-log search surface (#211): returns
        per-run rows AND global-chain rows (token issuance, OAuth sign-in,
        etc.) merged into one **time-descending** page. Same cursor +
        limit envelope as `/v0/runs/{run_id}/audit`. Optional `category`
        and `run_id` filters; combine for "show me approvals on this run."

        Distinct from `/v0/runs/{run_id}/audit` (sequence-ascending,
        scoped to one run, used by the run-detail audit list and the
        per-run verifier path). The global-chain partition specifically
        is the verifier's input — not exposed over HTTP for now.
      tags: [audit]
      parameters:
        - { name: category, in: query, schema: { type: string } }
        - { name: run_id, in: query, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - { name: limit, in: query, schema: { type: integer, default: 100, maximum: 500 } }
        - { name: cursor, in: query, schema: { type: string } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Page of audit entries, time-descending across both chains.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                allOf:
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Pagination'
                  - type: object
                    properties:
                      items:
                        type: array
                        items: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/AuditEntry' }
        '400':
          description: |
            `validation_failed` — `limit` out of range or `run_id` not a UUID;
            `cursor_invalid` — opaque cursor failed to decode.
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '503':
          description: Audit repository not configured on this instance.

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Work-item filing
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  /v0/work-items:
    post:
      operationId: fileWorkItem
      summary: File a work item via the repo's work-management conventions
      description: |
        Provider-agnostic work-item filing (#1005). Loads the repo's
        work-management conventions (the shipped default in v0; a per-repo
        `work_management` config in a follow-up), applies them to the
        caller's request — rendering the title from `title_format`,
        assembling the body from the type's skeleton + `sections`, merging
        `default_labels`, resolving board placement + complexity, allocating
        the sequential number for a numbered type (e.g. `adr`), and
        validating relations against the type's `epic_link` rule — then
        dispatches the resolved item to the configured provider
        (`github_projects`; `jira` is interface-only in v0) and returns the
        created item.

        `summary` is the mandatory one-liner: it fills the `{summary}`
        title placeholder and is the required Summary field. The same call
        shape works for any repo because only the conventions differ.

        **Epic auto-derivation.** For a child type whose `title_format`
        references `{epic}` (e.g. `[E{epic}.{n}]`), when `relations.parent_epic`
        is set and `title_vars` omits `epic`, the handler reads the parent epic
        issue's title server-side and parses its leading `[E<n>]` token into the
        `{epic}` placeholder — so the caller need only supply `{n}`. This fails
        closed: if no GitHub client/installation is available, the parent ref is
        unparseable, the issue fetch errors, or the parent title carries no
        `[E<n>]` token, `{epic}` is left unresolved and the `422` below is
        returned rather than a wrong title.

        **Sections fail loud.** When `body` is omitted, every `sections` key
        must match the type's `body_skeleton` exactly. An off-skeleton key
        returns `422 work_item_invalid` rather than silently dropping the
        content.

        When `run_id` names a run that is **in flight** (not terminal), a
        best-effort `work_item_filed` audit entry is appended to that run.
        Filing succeeds with no `run_id` (the operator-agent follow-up
        filing path, ADR-040) — `audited` is then `false`. An audit-append
        failure never fails the response: the item is already filed.

        On the run-absent path the handler resolves the App's installation
        for the target repo (`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/installation`) so
        GitHub filing works without a run; a repo the App is not installed
        on fails closed. The run-absent path is operator-only: a run-bound
        agent token must file run-scoped (supply its own `run_id`) and is
        rejected with `403 run_scoped_filing_required` if it omits `run_id`.

        An unregistered/unimplemented provider id fails closed with `501
        provider_unimplemented` naming the missing provider — never a panic.
      tags: [work-items]
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/WorkItemRequest' }
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Work item filed.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/WorkItemResponse' }
        '400':
          description: |
            `validation_failed` — `repo` not in owner/name form, or `type` /
            `summary` / `run_id` missing or malformed.
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            `run_not_entitled` — a `run_id` was supplied by a caller that is
            not that run's own run-bound agent token (`mcp:run:<uuid>`). Only
            the run's bound token may drive a `work_item_filed` audit entry
            onto its chain, so an arbitrary authenticated caller cannot inject
            an entry under their own actor_subject by knowing the run UUID.
            `run_repo_mismatch` — the entitled `run_id` belongs to a different
            repository than the filing target; a run_id is honoured only when
            its repo matches `repo`, so a run-bound token cannot borrow an
            unrelated repository's installation context.
        '404':
          description: "`run_not_found` — `run_id` supplied but no such run."
        '413':
          description: "`body_too_large` — request body exceeds the size cap."
        '422':
          description: |
            `work_item_invalid` — the request violates the conventions for
            its type (unknown type, missing complexity, unresolved title
            placeholder, an off-skeleton `sections` key, or an `epic_link`
            rule violation). Structured `details` accompany the relevant
            cases: `details.missing_placeholders` (the unresolved title
            placeholders, e.g. `["epic"]` when epic derivation failed closed)
            and `details.unknown_sections` / `details.expected_sections` (the
            off-skeleton key(s) and the type's skeleton section names).
        '501':
          description: |
            `provider_unimplemented` — the conventions name a provider that
            is not registered (e.g. `jira`); `details.provider` names it and
            `details.registered` lists the available providers.
        '502':
          description: "`work_item_filing_failed` — the provider rejected the filing."
        '503':
          description: "`run_lookup_unconfigured` — `run_id` supplied but no run repository is wired."

  /v0/calibration:
    get:
      operationId: getCalibration
      summary: Get runtime calibration statistics
      description: |
        Returns aggregated actual-vs-predicted runtime statistics derived
        from `runtime_observed` audit entries. Agents call this before writing
        a plan to self-correct `predicted_runtime_minutes` using
        `calibration_ratio` and `confidence_band_accuracy`.

        All parameters are optional. Omit them to get all-time stats across
        all implement stages.

        Zero `samples` is a valid response on a fresh installation.
      tags: [calibration]
      parameters:
        - name: workflow_id
          in: query
          description: Scope results to a specific workflow ID.
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: stage_type
          in: query
          description: Stage type to aggregate (default `implement`).
          schema: { type: string, default: implement }
        - name: since
          in: query
          description: RFC 3339 lower bound; omit for all-time stats.
          schema: { type: string, format: date-time }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Calibration statistics (samples may be 0).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/CalibrationResult' }
        '400':
          description: "`bad_since` — `since` is not a valid RFC 3339 timestamp."
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '503':
          description: Audit repository not configured on this instance.

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Stages, artifacts, approvals
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  /v0/stages/{stage_id}:
    get:
      operationId: getStage
      summary: Get a stage
      tags: [stages]
      parameters:
        - { name: stage_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Stage details.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Stage' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }

  /v0/stages/{stage_id}/artifacts:
    get:
      operationId: listStageArtifacts
      summary: List artifacts for a stage
      tags: [stages]
      parameters:
        - { name: stage_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Artifacts, ordered by created_at ascending.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                required: [items]
                properties:
                  items:
                    type: array
                    items: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Artifact' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }

  /v0/stages/{stage_id}/prompt:
    get:
      operationId: getStagePrompt
      summary: Fetch the constructed prompt for a stage
      description: |
        Returns the agent-facing prompt the runner should write to
        a temp file and pass to the executing agent's `--prompt-file`
        argument. Construction is server-side and deterministic so
        that two replays of the same stage produce byte-identical
        prompts and the audit log records exactly what the agent
        was asked to do (see MVP_SPEC §4.4).

        Auth is the same per-run signing-key scheme used by the
        trace upload endpoint. The runner signs
        `sha256("prompt:" + stage_id)` with the private half issued
        at signing-key time and sends the hex signature in
        `X-Fishhawk-Signature`.

        Returns 501 when no prompt template is implemented yet for
        the stage's type — caller should treat the stage as
        unsupported and fail rather than guess.
      tags: [stages]
      parameters:
        - { name: stage_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - in: header
          name: X-Fishhawk-Signature
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
            description: Hex-encoded Ed25519 signature of `sha256("prompt:" + stage_id)`.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Constructed prompt for the stage.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                required: [stage_id, stage_type, prompt, prompt_hash]
                properties:
                  stage_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
                  stage_type:
                    type: string
                    description: Stage type from the workflow spec (e.g. `implement`, `plan`).
                  prompt:
                    type: string
                    description: Plain-text prompt for the agent.
                  prompt_hash:
                    type: string
                    description: Hex-encoded sha256 of the prompt bytes.
                  agent_timeout_seconds:
                    type: integer
                    description: 'Wall-clock kill cap in seconds; 0 means the runner should apply its own fallback. For implement stages the value is dynamically widened above the spec budget to max(spec budget, approved-plan predicted_runtime_minutes x 2, implement-stage calibration p95 x 1.5), capped at 2x the spec budget; other stages return the spec-resolved value unchanged.'
                  decomposed_from_run_id:
                    type: string
                    format: uuid
                    description: Parent run ID when this run is a decomposed child. Absent for standalone runs. Runners use this with slice_index to route the child onto its own sole-writer slice branch fishhawk/run-<parent>/slice-<n> (ADR-041).
                  slice_index:
                    type: integer
                    description: Decomposed child's 0-based sub_plan position (ADR-041 / #1141), present only for decomposed children. The runner routes the child onto fishhawk/run-<shortID(decomposed_from_run_id)>/slice-<slice_index>. Omitted (treated as 0) for standalone runs and for slice 0.
                  verify_command:
                    type: string
                    description: Shell command for the verify gate; omitted when the spec declares none.
                  verify_timeout_seconds:
                    type: integer
                    description: Wall-clock cap for the verify command in seconds; 0 or absent means the runner applies its 10-minute fallback.
                  verify_max_iterations:
                    type: integer
                    description: Verify-fix loop budget from executor.verify.max_iterations; 0 or absent preserves the single-shot demote-on-failure gate, >0 caps the total verify-fix agent invocations across the stage.
                  min_runner_version:
                    type: string
                    description: Minimum runner version the backend requires; absent when the backend has no constraint. Runners older than this value must exit with code 3 (version_skew) rather than invoking the agent.
                  scope_files:
                    type: array
                    description: |
                      The approved plan's `scope.files`, echoed on implement
                      stages so the runner can bound the commit to exactly
                      these declared paths instead of `git add -A` (#581).
                      Absent when no approved plan is available
                      (`plan_missing_for_implement`) — the runner falls back
                      to staging every change. Dirty-but-undeclared paths are
                      excluded from the commit and flagged as scope drift
                      (non-blocking).
                    items:
                      type: object
                      required: [path, operation]
                      properties:
                        path:
                          type: string
                          description: Repo-relative path the agent declared it would touch.
                        operation:
                          type: string
                          enum: [create, modify, delete]
                          description: Per-file intent from the standard_v1 plan scope.files entry.
                  scope_exemptions:
                    type: array
                    description: |
                      Operator `exempt_scope_files` for a recovery run
                      (#1229), echoed on the implement stage so the runner's
                      `MissingScopeFiles` shortfall gate subtracts each
                      operator-justified-unchanged declared path — the inverse
                      of `add_scope_files` (it subtracts from the gate, it does
                      not widen scope). Resolved from the run's own
                      `plan_reused_from` provenance; absent on every
                      non-recovery run.
                    items:
                      type: object
                      required: [path, reason]
                      properties:
                        path:
                          type: string
                          description: Repo-relative declared scope.files path marked operator-justified-unchanged.
                        reason:
                          type: string
                          description: Operator justification for leaving the declared path unchanged.
                  implement_model:
                    type: string
                    description: |
                      The backend-resolved implement model id (#1013), echoed
                      on implement stages so the runner pins the agent spawn to
                      it (`--model <implement_model>`). Resolved through the
                      implement-model ladder: operator gate decision < plan
                      `model_recommendation.implement_model` < spec
                      `executor.model` < deployment default. Absent when no rung
                      supplied a model — the runner leaves the model unset and
                      the agent spawns on the adapter's built-in default,
                      byte-identical to today.
                  commit_author_name:
                    type: string
                    description: |
                      The GitHub App bot account's git author name
                      (`<app-slug>[bot]`), resolved backend-side from the App
                      so App-backed commits attribute to the App's bot account
                      (#722). Absent when the backend can't resolve it (no App
                      JWT, dev/CLI) — the runner falls back to its default bot
                      identity.
                  commit_author_email:
                    type: string
                    description: |
                      The GitHub App bot account's git author email
                      (`<bot-user-id>+<app-slug>[bot]@users.noreply.github.com`),
                      paired with `commit_author_name` (#722). Absent under the
                      same conditions; the runner falls back to its default.
                  fixup:
                    type: boolean
                    description: |
                      True when this implement stage is an operator-triggered
                      implement-review fix-up pass (#762/#784): an unconsumed
                      `stage_fixup_triggered` audit entry routed review concerns
                      back to this stage. The runner takes its same-branch
                      `RebaseFromRemote` path (fetch + checkout + pull --rebase
                      on `fixup_branch`) and updates the open PR rather than
                      opening a new one. Absent on a normal implement dispatch —
                      branch routing falls through to the per-stage branch.
                  fixup_branch:
                    type: string
                    description: |
                      The existing PR branch a fix-up pass commits onto. Present
                      only when `fixup` is true. Derived to match the runner's
                      branch formula exactly: non-decomposed
                      `fishhawk/run-<shortID(run_id)>/stage-<shortID(stage_id)>`,
                      decomposed `fishhawk/run-<shortID(decomposed_from_run_id)>`
                      (shortID = first 8 chars of the UUID). A divergence would
                      re-create the `checkout -b <existing branch>` failure
                      (#784).
                  fixup_expected_head_sha:
                    type: string
                    description: |
                      The run's recorded head SHA — the newest `head_sha`
                      across the run's reported-head ledger entries
                      (`pull_request_opened` / `child_pushed` /
                      `fixup_pushed`), the same audit source the ADR-035
                      branch-lineage verifier treats as the run's legitimate
                      tips. Present only when `fixup` is true and resolution
                      succeeded. Before invoking the agent the runner fetches
                      + checks out `fixup_branch` and fails fast when the
                      fetched tip differs from this value (#967). Absent
                      (older backend, or backend-side resolution failure)
                      means the runner proceeds with checkout only, skipping
                      the comparison.
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '409':
          description: Stage is not in a runnable state (awaiting_approval, awaiting_children, succeeded, failed, or cancelled).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                allOf:
                  - { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
                  - description: |
                      `error.code` is `stage_not_runnable`.
                      `error.details.current_state` holds the stage's current state string.
                      `error.details.stage_id` holds the stage UUID.
        '501':
          description: Prompt construction not implemented for this stage type.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503':
          description: Backend wiring incomplete (run, signing, or GitHub repo unconfigured).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/stages/{stage_id}/prompt-render:
    get:
      operationId: getStagePromptRender
      summary: Fetch the constructed prompt for a stage (SPA-readable)
      description: |
        SPA-side counterpart of `getStagePrompt` (#215). Returns the
        same body shape, built by the same deterministic
        `prompt.Build` pipeline, without requiring
        `X-Fishhawk-Signature`. Read access tracks the existing
        stage / audit reads.

        The two endpoints exist as siblings rather than one with a
        toggle so the runner contract on the signature-authed path
        stays unchanged when the SPA-side surface evolves.
      tags: [stages]
      parameters:
        - { name: stage_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Constructed prompt for the stage.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                required: [stage_id, stage_type, prompt, prompt_hash]
                properties:
                  stage_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
                  stage_type: { type: string }
                  prompt: { type: string }
                  prompt_hash: { type: string }
                  agent_timeout_seconds:
                    type: integer
                    description: 'Wall-clock kill cap in seconds; 0 means the runner should apply its own fallback. For implement stages the value is dynamically widened above the spec budget to max(spec budget, approved-plan predicted_runtime_minutes x 2, implement-stage calibration p95 x 1.5), capped at 2x the spec budget; other stages return the spec-resolved value unchanged.'
                  decomposed_from_run_id:
                    type: string
                    format: uuid
                    description: Parent run ID when this run is a decomposed child. Absent for standalone runs.
                  slice_index:
                    type: integer
                    description: Decomposed child's 0-based sub_plan position (ADR-041 / #1141), present only for decomposed children. The runner routes the child onto fishhawk/run-<shortID(decomposed_from_run_id)>/slice-<slice_index>. Omitted (treated as 0) for standalone runs and for slice 0.
                  verify_command:
                    type: string
                    description: Shell command for the verify gate; omitted when the spec declares none.
                  verify_timeout_seconds:
                    type: integer
                    description: Wall-clock cap for the verify command in seconds; 0 or absent means the runner applies its 10-minute fallback.
                  verify_max_iterations:
                    type: integer
                    description: Verify-fix loop budget from executor.verify.max_iterations; 0 or absent preserves the single-shot demote-on-failure gate, >0 caps the total verify-fix agent invocations across the stage.
                  min_runner_version:
                    type: string
                    description: Minimum runner version the backend requires; absent when the backend has no constraint.
                  scope_files:
                    type: array
                    description: |
                      The approved plan's `scope.files`, echoed on implement
                      stages so the runner can bound the commit to exactly
                      these declared paths instead of `git add -A` (#581).
                      Absent when no approved plan is available.
                    items:
                      type: object
                      required: [path, operation]
                      properties:
                        path:
                          type: string
                          description: Repo-relative path the agent declared it would touch.
                        operation:
                          type: string
                          enum: [create, modify, delete]
                          description: Per-file intent from the standard_v1 plan scope.files entry.
                  scope_exemptions:
                    type: array
                    description: |
                      Operator `exempt_scope_files` for a recovery run
                      (#1229), echoed on the implement stage so the runner's
                      `MissingScopeFiles` shortfall gate subtracts each
                      operator-justified-unchanged declared path — the inverse
                      of `add_scope_files` (it subtracts from the gate, it does
                      not widen scope). Resolved from the run's own
                      `plan_reused_from` provenance; absent on every
                      non-recovery run.
                    items:
                      type: object
                      required: [path, reason]
                      properties:
                        path:
                          type: string
                          description: Repo-relative declared scope.files path marked operator-justified-unchanged.
                        reason:
                          type: string
                          description: Operator justification for leaving the declared path unchanged.
                  implement_model:
                    type: string
                    description: Backend-resolved implement model id (#1013), echoed on implement stages so the runner pins the agent spawn to it (`--model <implement_model>`). Resolved through the implement-model ladder (operator gate decision < plan `model_recommendation.implement_model` < spec `executor.model` < deployment default). Absent when no rung supplied a model — the runner leaves the model unset and the agent spawns on the adapter's built-in default, byte-identical to today.
                  commit_author_name:
                    type: string
                    description: GitHub App bot account's git author name (`<app-slug>[bot]`), resolved backend-side (#722). Absent when unresolvable; the runner falls back to its default.
                  commit_author_email:
                    type: string
                    description: GitHub App bot account's git author email (`<bot-user-id>+<app-slug>[bot]@users.noreply.github.com`), paired with commit_author_name (#722).
                  fixup:
                    type: boolean
                    description: True when this implement stage is an operator-triggered implement-review fix-up pass (#762/#784); the runner commits onto `fixup_branch` via its same-branch RebaseFromRemote path and updates the open PR instead of opening a new one. Absent on a normal implement dispatch.
                  fixup_branch:
                    type: string
                    description: Existing PR branch a fix-up pass commits onto; present only when `fixup` is true. Matches the runner's branch formula — non-decomposed `fishhawk/run-<shortID(run_id)>/stage-<shortID(stage_id)>`, decomposed `fishhawk/run-<shortID(decomposed_from_run_id)>` (#784).
                  fixup_expected_head_sha:
                    type: string
                    description: The run's recorded head SHA — the newest `head_sha` across the run's reported-head ledger entries (`pull_request_opened` / `child_pushed` / `fixup_pushed`), the ADR-035 lineage source. Present only when `fixup` is true and resolution succeeded; the runner verifies the fetched `fixup_branch` tip against it before invoking the agent (#967).
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '409':
          description: Stage is not in a runnable state (awaiting_approval, awaiting_children, succeeded, failed, or cancelled).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                allOf:
                  - { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
                  - description: |
                      `error.code` is `stage_not_runnable`.
                      `error.details.current_state` holds the stage's current state string.
                      `error.details.stage_id` holds the stage UUID.
        '501':
          description: Prompt construction not implemented for this stage type.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503':
          description: Backend wiring incomplete (run repo or GitHub unconfigured).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/stages/{stage_id}/trace:
    get:
      operationId: getStageTrace
      summary: Stream the redacted trace bundle for a stage
      description: |
        Streams the most-recent **redacted** trace bundle the runner
        shipped for this stage. The body is gzipped JSON Lines; the
        SPA gunzips client-side (or the browser auto-decompresses
        when `Accept-Encoding: gzip` is honored) and parses
        line-by-line to render the agent transcript (#218).

        The bundle's first line is the manifest (agent, model,
        agent_failed flag); middle lines are one captured agent
        event each; last line is the trailer with event count + a
        sha256 of the preceding lines (truncation detector). Schema
        is ADR-007 (#71).

        Raw variant access is not exposed here. The raw bundle can
        carry secrets in agent output and lives behind S3 Object
        Lock for compliance reasons; surfacing it is a separate
        decision.
      tags: [stages]
      parameters:
        - { name: stage_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            Gzipped JSON Lines bundle bytes. `X-Fishhawk-Content-Hash`
            carries the bundle's hex sha256 so callers can cross-
            reference the audit log's `trace_uploaded` payload.
          headers:
            X-Fishhawk-Content-Hash:
              schema: { type: string }
              description: Hex sha256 of the gzipped bundle bytes.
            Content-Encoding:
              schema: { type: string, enum: [gzip] }
          content:
            application/x-ndjson:
              schema:
                type: string
                format: binary
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest' }
        '404':
          description: |
            `stage_not_found` — no stage with that id;
            `trace_not_found` — stage exists but no redacted bundle
            has been uploaded yet.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '410':
          description: |
            `trace_storage_missing` — the audit log references a
            bundle that's no longer in storage (lifecycle expiry,
            manual deletion, or a storage outage). The audit chain
            is the canonical record; the storage gap needs human /
            verifier intervention.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503':
          description: Backend wiring incomplete (run, audit, or tracestore unconfigured).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/stages/{stage_id}/checks:
    get:
      operationId: listStageChecks
      summary: Latest state per blocking check on a stage
      description: |
        Returns the most-recent observed state for each required
        check on the stage's run (#228). `declared` is the run's
        required-checks snapshot from GitHub branch protection
        (#251 / ADR-017) — pre-#254 it sourced from the spec-level
        `gate.blocking_checks` field, which has been dropped. `items`
        is the latest observed state per check name. Declared-but-
        not-observed checks appear in `declared` but not `items` —
        the SPA fills them with `not_tracked`.

        State values per check are the SPA-facing rollup of GitHub's
        `status` + `conclusion` pair, or the backend's self-derived
        equivalent for `fishhawk_audit_complete` (#229): `pass`,
        `fail`, `pending`, or `not_tracked`.

        For `fishhawk_audit_complete` the response item additionally
        carries a `missing` array naming what's incomplete (plan
        artifact, raw or redacted trace bundle, pull-request
        artifact, or audit-chain mismatch) so the SPA can render
        the failure reason without a secondary call. The check is
        computed on read from artifact + audit-log presence; no row
        for it lands in `stage_checks`. The audit-complete row is
        injected only for review stages.
      tags: [stages]
      parameters:
        - { name: stage_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Declared required checks + latest observed state per check.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                required: [declared, sources, items]
                properties:
                  declared:
                    type: array
                    items: { type: string }
                    description: |
                      Required-status-check context names from the
                      run's branch-protection snapshot (#251). Empty
                      when the run pre-dates the snapshot wiring or
                      skipped protection lookup (CLI / UI).
                  sources:
                    type: array
                    items: { type: string }
                    description: |
                      Surfaces that contributed to `declared`. Each
                      entry is `branch_protection` or `ruleset:<id>`.
                      The SPA renders this as a "Required by branch
                      protection" sub-label so reviewers know where
                      the list comes from (#256).
                  items:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      type: object
                      required: [name, state]
                      properties:
                        name: { type: string }
                        state:
                          type: string
                          enum: [pass, fail, pending, not_tracked]
                        status:
                          type: string
                          description: Verbatim GitHub `check_run.status` (queued / in_progress / completed).
                        conclusion:
                          type: [string, "null"]
                          description: Verbatim GitHub `check_run.conclusion`; null while not completed.
                        head_sha:
                          type: string
                        github_check_run_id:
                          type: [integer, "null"]
                          format: int64
                        ts:
                          type: string
                          format: date-time
                        missing:
                          type: array
                          description: |
                            Populated only on the `fishhawk_audit_complete`
                            row when the derivation found gaps. Each entry
                            names a category of incompleteness; consumers
                            branch on `kind`. Absent / empty when the check
                            passes or for externally-fed checks like
                            `ci_pass`.
                          items:
                            type: object
                            required: [kind, detail]
                            properties:
                              kind:
                                type: string
                                enum:
                                  - plan_missing
                                  - trace_missing
                                  - pr_missing
                                  - chain_invalid
                                  - chain_unrecoverable
                                  - foreign_commit
                                  - head_fetch_failed
                                  - review_pending
                              detail:
                                type: string
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '503':
          description: Backend wiring incomplete (run repo or stage-check repo unconfigured).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/stages/{stage_id}/approvals:
    post:
      operationId: submitApproval
      summary: Approve or reject the gate on a stage
      description: |
        The authenticated user must resolve to a role listed in the
        gate's `approvers` block. Submitting an `approve` decision
        transitions the gate to passed; `reject` fails the stage as
        category D.

        Requires `write:approvals` scope. A **deploy-stage** approve
        additionally requires `write:deploy` (ADR-038 / #1390): the deploy
        gate's `approve` is the pre-execution authorization for a real release,
        so an operator bearer holding `write:approvals` but missing
        `write:deploy` is refused 403 `insufficient_scope`
        (`required_scope: write:deploy`) before the deploy pre-flight runs. The
        `write:deploy` requirement applies to the deploy `approve` path ONLY —
        a deploy `reject` (the reviewer judgment that always pages the human)
        and every plan/implement/review approval are unaffected. Deploy
        approvers are enforced through the same `approvers` block; an `all_of`
        deploy gate requires the subject to belong to EVERY named role.
      tags: [approvals]
      parameters:
        - { name: stage_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [decision]
              properties:
                decision:
                  type: string
                  enum: [approve, reject]
                comment:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional free-text rationale. On `approve` it is
                    recorded as the #558 binding approval conditions and
                    injected verbatim into the implement (and
                    implement-review) prompt as instructions the agent
                    must follow. It is NOT parsed for scope: repo-relative
                    paths written in the comment are NO LONGER folded into
                    the implement stage's `scope.files` (#1225 removed the
                    #730 prose fold). Use `add_scope_files` to add scope —
                    naming a path here to explain it (e.g. "no edit to
                    `backend/go.mod` is needed") will not mutate scope.
                approver_github_login:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional resolved GitHub login of the acting
                    operator, threaded through by the MCP
                    approve/reject tools (#751). Used only for
                    issue-thread `@`-mention rendering in the
                    plan-status footer; the audit `approver` field
                    keeps the token subject as the acting identity
                    (provenance). SPA/CLI callers omit it and are
                    unaffected. `decision` remains the only required
                    field.
                add_scope_files:
                  type: array
                  items:
                    type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional, authoritative list of repo-relative
                    paths to fold into the implement stage's
                    `scope.files` on approve (#824). This is now the SOLE
                    source of approve-time `scope.files` additions: the
                    #730 free-text `comment` prose fold was removed
                    (#1225), so repo-relative paths written in `comment`
                    no longer mutate scope. The structured, lossless
                    form succeeds where a regex scrape failed — it stages
                    directories, extensionless or repo-root files (e.g.
                    `go.work`), and described-but-not-spelled paths. A
                    trailing slash marks a directory whose created files
                    all stage under it (e.g. `pkg/testdata/corpus/`).
                    Recorded on the approval audit payload and consumed by
                    the prompt builder. No-op when the plan declares an
                    empty scope (preserves the runner's `git add -A`
                    fallback). Does NOT weaken the policy gate: a folded
                    path matching `forbidden_paths` still fails category-B
                    against the produced diff. Honored only on `approve`;
                    ignored on `reject`.
                binding_assertions:
                  type: array
                  items:
                    type: object
                    required: [type, path, literal]
                    properties:
                      type:
                        type: string
                        enum: [file_contains, test_asserts]
                        description: |
                          The assertion type. `file_contains` checks the
                          literal appears in the committed file at `path`;
                          `test_asserts` is the identical substring check but
                          `path` must name a Go test file. Open enum — an
                          unknown type is rejected `400 validation_failed`.
                      path:
                        type: string
                        description: |
                          Repo-relative path of the committed file the
                          literal must appear in. For `test_asserts` it must
                          end in `_test.go`.
                      literal:
                        type: string
                        description: |
                          The non-empty substring that must be present in the
                          committed content of `path` (deterministic match).
                  description: |
                    Optional list of operator-declared, deterministic
                    binding-assertion checks (#1171) — the machine-checkable
                    half of an approval condition. Each typed substring
                    assertion is recorded on the approval audit payload
                    alongside `add_scope_files`, echoed on the implement
                    prompt-response, and evaluated by the runner
                    post-implement against the committed scope-only tree; any
                    unsatisfied assertion fails the implement stage
                    category-B. Substring matching only — never parses prose.
                    A malformed declaration (unknown `type`, empty `literal`,
                    a `test_asserts` `path` not ending in `_test.go`) is
                    rejected `400 validation_failed` before any approval row
                    is recorded. Honored only on `approve`; ignored on
                    `reject`. Omitting the field is byte-identical to today.
                implement_model:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional operator override for the implement-stage model
                    (#1013) — the top rung of the resolution ladder
                    (deployment default < spec `executor.model` < plan
                    `model_recommendation` < this override). On a plan-stage
                    approve the gate resolves the full ladder with this as the
                    operator rung, validates the RESOLVED non-empty value
                    against the deployment's per-adapter allow-list, and emits a
                    `model_resolved` audit entry (`{model, model_source}`) the
                    runner spawn routes to the agent `--model`. An unknown
                    resolved model — from ANY rung, not just this field — is
                    rejected `422 plan_invalid_model` (details carry `model`,
                    `model_source`, `adapter`) PRE-Submit, so a retry with an
                    allowed model flows normally. An empty/unconfigured
                    allow-list fails OPEN (any model accepted, byte-identical to
                    today). Honored only on `approve`; ignored on `reject`.
                    Omitting the field leaves the model to the lower rungs; an
                    empty resolution still records `model_resolved` and spawns
                    with no `--model`, exactly as today.
                plan_model:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional operator override for the PLAN-stage model (#1416)
                    — the top rung of the plan-model ladder (deployment default
                    < spec `executor.model` on the plan stage < this override).
                    On a plan-stage approve the gate resolves the plan ladder
                    with this as the operator rung and emits the plan stage's
                    `model_resolved` audit entry (`{model, model_source,
                    stage_type: plan}`); a re-dispatched plan stage then spawns
                    under the resolved value. Honored only on `approve`; ignored
                    on `reject`. Omitting the field leaves the plan model to the
                    lower rungs, byte-identical to today.
                review_model:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional operator override for the REVIEW-stage model
                    (#1416) — the top rung of the review-model ladder
                    (deployment default < spec `executor.model` on the review
                    stage < this override). On a plan-stage approve the gate
                    resolves the review ladder with this as the operator rung and
                    emits the review stage's `model_resolved` audit entry
                    (`{model, model_source, stage_type: review}`); the
                    post-plan-gate implement review (and any post-gate
                    re-review) then invokes each heterogeneous reviewer under the
                    resolved value. It governs the implement review, NOT the
                    already-completed plan review. Honored only on `approve`;
                    ignored on `reject`. Omitting the field leaves each reviewer
                    on its spec model, byte-identical to today.
                delegated:
                  type: boolean
                  default: false
                  description: |
                    Opts the submission into the ADR-040 delegated-action
                    path (#1026): the caller (the operator agent) asserts
                    it acts under the workflow's
                    `operator_agent.may_approve` knob. The server NEVER
                    trusts the assertion — it re-evaluates the knob's
                    condition (`clean_dual_approval`) against current run
                    state at action time. Refusals are 403:
                    `delegation_not_configured` when no effective
                    `operator_agent` block delegates the approve action
                    (fail-closed — including legacy runs with no cached
                    spec), `delegation_condition_unmet` with the named
                    failed predicate in `details.unmet_reason` otherwise.
                    A refusal inserts no approval row, so a later retry
                    flows normally. When met, the approval proceeds and
                    its `approval_submitted` audit payload records
                    `delegated: "clean_dual_approval"`. Only valid with
                    `decision: approve` — a delegated reject is 400
                    (rejection is a human judgment; `reviewer_reject`
                    pages the human). Omitted → behavior byte-identical
                    to a human submission.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            Two distinct outcomes share this status (#986):

            - **Approval recorded; stage state advanced.** The body is
              the bare `Stage` shape; the three duplicate fields are
              omitted.
            - **Duplicate submission** — the same authenticated subject
              already submitted a decision for this stage. The prior
              decision stands, the stage state is unchanged, NO gates
              (budget/scope-cap/review-settled) re-run, and no audit
              entries are emitted. The body carries
              `duplicate_submission: true` plus `prior_decision` and
              `prior_submitted_at` from the EXISTING approval row
              (authentic provenance, not echoes of the new request).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                allOf:
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Stage'
                  - type: object
                    properties:
                      duplicate_submission:
                        type: boolean
                        description: |
                          Present (true) only when this submission was a
                          no-op duplicate of an existing decision by the
                          same subject. Omitted on a first submission.
                      prior_decision:
                        type: string
                        enum: [approve, reject]
                        description: |
                          The existing approval row's decision. Only
                          present alongside `duplicate_submission`.
                      prior_submitted_at:
                        type: string
                        format: date-time
                        description: |
                          When the existing approval row was submitted.
                          Only present alongside `duplicate_submission`.
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            Possible error codes:

            - `approver_not_authorized` — a RoleResolver is configured
              and the subject is not in the gate's approvers list. For an
              `all_of` deploy gate this fires unless the subject belongs to
              EVERY named role.
            - `insufficient_scope` — a **deploy-stage** `approve` from an
              operator bearer that holds `write:approvals` but is missing
              `write:deploy` (ADR-038 / #1390). `details.required_scope` is
              `write:deploy`. Applies to the deploy `approve` path only.
            - `delegation_not_configured` — the request carried
              `delegated: true` but no effective `operator_agent` block
              delegates the approve action (ADR-040 / #1026,
              fail-closed): the spec declares no block, the block has no
              `may_approve` knob, or the run has no cached workflow
              spec.
            - `delegation_condition_unmet` — `delegated: true` and the
              `may_approve` knob is configured, but its condition is not
              satisfied by current run state. `details` carries
              `action`, `condition`, and `unmet_reason` naming the exact
              failed predicate (e.g. `clean_dual_approval: 1 of 2
              reviewer verdicts received`).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '409':
          description: |
            Possible error codes:

            - `invalid_state_transition` — stage isn't in
              awaiting_approval. (Pre-#253 the handler also returned
              409 with `blocking_checks_not_passed` when any declared
              blocking check wasn't `pass`. ADR-017 / #249 moved that
              gate to GitHub branch protection; the approval API no
              longer reads stage_check state.)
            - `review_stage_managed_by_github` — the target stage is
              a review stage. ADR-018 / #311 moved review-stage
              approval onto GitHub: the `pull_request.closed`
              webhook with `merged=true` transitions the stage;
              `pull_request_review.submitted` events feed the audit
              log. Branch protection's required-reviewers is the
              approver-list mechanism. Response body's `details`
              includes the run's `pull_request_url` when stamped so
              the caller knows where the merge gate lives. Plan-
              stage approvals continue to use this endpoint.
            - `agent_review_pending` — ADR-036 / #875: the target is
              a plan stage whose workflow declares `reviewers.agent`
              > 0, a plan review was dispatched
              (`plan_review_started` present), and fewer than the
              configured agent count of TERMINAL review verdicts
              (`plan_reviewed` / `plan_review_failed` /
              `plan_review_skipped`) have landed. The approval is
              refused before any row is recorded — poll until the
              review reaches a terminal state, then retry; the retry
              is idempotent. `details` includes `stage_id`,
              `configured_agents`, and `landed_terminal`. A hard
              backstop (`ReviewBudget.Cap` × configured agents,
              measured from the earliest `plan_review_started`)
              allows the approval once elapsed even if no terminal
              verdict landed, recording a
              `plan_review_backstop_elapsed` audit entry — so a
              reviewer that dies emitting nothing can never strand
              the gate.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '422':
          description: |
            Possible error codes:

            - `plan_violates_budget` — the plan's
              `predicted_runtime_minutes` exceeds the resolved
              implement-stage budget and neither a
              `decomposition.sub_plans` block nor an
              `--override-budget` marker in the comment is present.
              The budget is the spec-resolved implement-stage timeout
              widened to the calibration p95 × 1.5 when implement
              runtime data exists, clamped to spec × 2 (#994) — the
              same base the dynamic implement kill cap builds on, so
              gate and runtime cannot disagree. `details` includes
              `predicted_minutes`, `budget_minutes` (the RESOLVED
              budget), `budget_source` (`spec`, `p95`, or `ceiling` —
              which term won the resolution), `spec_budget_minutes`
              (the raw spec-resolved floor), and `timeout_source`
              (`stage_executor_timeout`,
              `workflow_policy_max_stage_runtime`, or
              `backend_default` — the spec value's provenance).
              Refused BEFORE any approval row is recorded (#986), so
              the same subject's retry with the override flows
              normally rather than dead-ending as an idempotent
              duplicate. To unblock: either re-plan with a
              decomposition block, or re-submit the approval with
              `--override-budget` in the comment.
            - `plan_violates_scope_cap` — #983: the effective scope —
              the plan's `scope.files` unioned with the request's
              `add_scope_files`, prior approve-time folds, and
              already-approved scope amendments, deduped by exact
              path — exceeds the implement stage's resolved
              `max_files_changed` and the comment lacks an
              `--override-scope-cap` marker. Refused BEFORE any
              approval row is recorded, so a retry after re-scoping
              (or carrying the override) flows normally. `details`
              includes `scoped_files`, `max_files_changed`, and
              `add_scope_files_count`; the refusal appends a
              `plan_violates_scope_cap` audit entry, the override a
              `plan_scope_cap_override_acknowledged` entry. Declared
              scope is an upper bound on the eventual diff, so the
              override exists for legitimately-forced cases (e.g. the
              cap itself is about to change).
            - `plan_invalid_model` — #1013: the RESOLVED implement model
              (the ladder of deployment default < spec `executor.model` <
              plan `model_recommendation` < the `implement_model` override)
              is non-empty and not in the deployment's per-adapter
              allow-list for the run's implement adapter. Validated regardless
              of which rung supplied the value, so an unknown plan- or
              spec-recommended model — not just an operator override — is
              caught here rather than at runner spawn. `details` includes
              `model`, `model_source` (`default`|`spec`|`plan`|`operator`),
              and `adapter`. Refused BEFORE any approval row is recorded, so a
              retry with an allowed `implement_model` (or after widening the
              allow-list) flows normally. An empty/unconfigured allow-list
              fails OPEN — any model is accepted, byte-identical to today.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/stages/{stage_id}/clarification:
    post:
      operationId: answerClarification
      summary: Answer a plan stage parked at awaiting_input
      description: |
        Closes the #1057 answer-and-resume seam (#1088). A plan stage the
        planner parked at `awaiting_input` with a `clarification_request`
        (#1080) is stranded until the operator answers: nothing populates the
        answers or re-opens the stage. This endpoint validates the operator's
        answers against the questions the planner actually parked, persists
        them as a DEDICATED `clarification_answered` audit entry, transitions
        the stage `awaiting_input` → `pending`, and hands off to the
        orchestrator so a drive run re-dispatches the plan stage (a local run
        re-runs via the operator's next `fishhawk_run_stage plan`). The resumed
        plan prompt injects the answers in its binding "Clarification answers"
        section.

        The `clarification_answered` channel is intentionally isolated from the
        approval (`approval_submitted` / `decision=approve`) channel: a parked
        plan stage is NOT yet approved, and the implement prompt's approval-
        conditions loader matches only `decision=='approve'`, so the two never
        collide.

        Requires `write:approvals` scope — the #558 binding-conditions /
        gate-answer family.
      tags: [approvals]
      parameters:
        - { name: stage_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [answers]
              properties:
                answers:
                  type: array
                  minItems: 1
                  description: |
                    The operator's answers, each keyed by a parked question
                    `id`. Every id must match a question in the newest
                    `clarification_requested` entry, every parked question must
                    be answered, and ids must not repeat.
                  items:
                    type: object
                    required: [id, answer]
                    properties:
                      id:
                        type: string
                        description: The parked question's stable id.
                      answer:
                        type: string
                        description: The operator's answer to that question.
                comment:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional free-text note appended after the rendered answers
                    in the binding-conditions blob the resume prompt injects.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            Answers recorded; the plan stage transitioned
            `awaiting_input` → `pending`. The body is the bare `Stage` shape.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Stage' }
        '400':
          description: |
            Possible error codes:

            - `validation_failed` — the body is not valid JSON, carries
              unknown fields, or `answers` is empty.
            - `clarification_answer_invalid` — an answer id does not match any
              parked question, a parked question was left unanswered, or an id
              was repeated. The answers are keyed to real parked questions, so
              an ambiguous or incomplete set is refused before anything is
              recorded.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '409':
          description: |
            `invalid_state_transition` — the target is not a plan stage parked
            at `awaiting_input` (the only state with parked questions to
            answer), or the `awaiting_input` → `pending` transition was
            rejected by the state machine. `details` carries `stage_id`,
            `stage_type`, and `stage_state`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/stages/{stage_id}/revise:
    post:
      operationId: revisePlan
      summary: Re-plan a plan stage in place against a binding operator constraint
      description: |
        The plan-gate `revise` verdict (#1099) — the third option alongside
        approve and reject. A `revise` re-plans IN PLACE in the same run: it
        re-opens the plan stage parked at `awaiting_approval` back to
        `pending`, re-dispatches the plan stage ONCE with the operator's
        binding design `constraint` injected into the plan prompt (a DEDICATED
        "Revision constraint" section — the #558 binding-conditions channel)
        and the prior plan carried as the revision base, then re-enters the
        normal plan review → approve gate. It is the plan-stage analogue of
        `POST /v0/stages/{stage_id}/fixup` (a bounded, operator-gated re-open
        of a HEALTHY gate).

        Use `revise` when the plan's direction is sound but a design
        constraint must change first — cheaper than rejecting the plan to a
        fresh-run replan, because only the constrained parts change.

        Bounded + operator-gated: the NORMAL bound defaults to ONE revise pass
        per stage, enforced server-side by counting prior `plan_revised` audit
        entries (no dedicated column — exactly as fix-up counts
        `stage_fixup_triggered`). Once spent, an operator may grant ONE more
        pass with `force_additional_pass: true`, hard-capped at 3 TOTAL passes
        per stage; at the ceiling the endpoint refuses with the DISTINCT
        `revise_ceiling_reached`. A run-bound MCP token may revise only stages
        within its own run.

        Requires `write:approvals` scope — the #558 binding-conditions /
        gate-answer family.
      tags: [approvals]
      parameters:
        - { name: stage_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [constraint]
              properties:
                constraint:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    The binding design constraint the planner must revise the
                    prior plan to satisfy. Injected into the re-dispatched plan
                    prompt as a dedicated, binding "Revision constraint"
                    section (MANDATORY, wins on conflict), with the prior plan
                    as the revision base. Empty/whitespace-only is rejected.
                force_additional_pass:
                  type: boolean
                  default: false
                  description: |
                    Bounded operator override: grant ONE revise pass beyond the
                    normal budget (default 1) when it is already spent, hard-
                    capped at 3 total passes per stage. The forced pass is
                    audited on the `plan_revised` entry (`forced: true`).
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            The plan stage was re-opened `awaiting_approval` → `pending` and
            the orchestrator re-dispatches it. The body is the re-opened
            `Stage` shape (pending, or dispatched once the orchestrator
            advances it before the response returns).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Stage' }
        '400':
          description: |
            `validation_failed` — the `stage_id` is not a valid UUID, the body
            is not valid JSON, or `constraint` is empty/whitespace-only.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            Possible error codes:

            - `insufficient_scope` — the token lacks `write:approvals`.
            - `cross_run_revise` — a run-bound MCP token tried to revise a
              stage outside its own run.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '409':
          description: |
            Possible error codes:

            - `revise_not_applicable` — the target is not a plan stage parked
              at `awaiting_approval`. `details` carries `stage_type` and
              `stage_state`.
            - `revise_budget_exhausted` — the NORMAL bounded pass count is
              spent; one more pass is available via `force_additional_pass`.
              `details` carries `max_passes` and `used`.
            - `revise_ceiling_reached` — the hard ceiling of 3 total passes is
              reached; the override cannot push past it (reject → fresh-run
              replan). `details` carries `ceiling` and `used`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
  /v0/stages/{stage_id}/retry:
    post:
      operationId: retryStage
      summary: Retry a failed stage
      description: |
        Re-opens a failed stage when the failure category supports it.
        Per MVP_SPEC §6:

        - **D, sla_timeout**: transitions failed → awaiting_approval,
          clears failure metadata, restarts the SLA clock implicitly
          via the `updated_at` trigger. Returns 200 with the updated stage.
        - **A** / **C**: transitions failed → pending, then the
          orchestrator walks pending → dispatched and fires
          workflow_dispatch. Returns 200 with the post-orchestrator
          stage (typically `dispatched`). Orchestrator failures are
          logged but don't fail the request — the stage stays at
          pending and an operator can re-fire Advance manually.
        - **D, gate-rejected**: 422 (`retry_not_applicable`) — the
          approver said no; create a fresh run instead.
        - **B**: 422 (`retry_not_applicable`) — the workflow or spec
          needs to change before a retry would behave differently,
          UNLESS the request carries `{override: true, reason: "..."}`
          (see request body below).

        The optional request body enables an **audited category-B
        override** (#698). With `{override: true}` and a non-empty
        `reason`, a genuine category-B failure is admitted onto the same
        failed → pending re-open path as A/C: the stage RE-RUNS and the
        policy gate RE-EVALUATES the new diff. The override does NOT
        accept the B-violating diff or bypass the gate — it only lets the
        stage run again. `reason` is mandatory when `override` is true
        (400 `validation_failed` otherwise). A successful override writes
        a distinct `stage_override_retried` audit entry (actor + reason +
        prior category B), kept separate from the ordinary `stage_retried`
        receipt. Without an override the body may be omitted entirely and
        default per-category semantics apply, writing `stage_retried`.
      tags: [stages]
      parameters:
        - { name: stage_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: false
        description: |
          Optional. Omit for a default per-category retry. Supply
          `{override: true, reason: "..."}` to invoke the audited
          category-B override.
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              additionalProperties: false
              properties:
                override:
                  type: boolean
                  default: false
                  description: |
                    Admit a category-B failure onto the failed → pending
                    re-open path. The stage re-runs and the policy gate
                    re-evaluates; the gate is not bypassed.
                reason:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Operator justification, recorded in the
                    `stage_override_retried` audit entry. Required when
                    `override` is true.
                delegated:
                  type: boolean
                  default: false
                  description: |
                    Opts the retry into the ADR-040 delegated-action path
                    (#1026). The server re-evaluates the
                    `operator_agent.may_retry` condition (`infra_flake`)
                    against current run state at action time: 403
                    `delegation_not_configured` when no effective block
                    delegates the retry action (fail-closed), 403
                    `delegation_condition_unmet` with the named failed
                    predicate when the latest failure is not a
                    category-A infra flake. When met, the retry proceeds
                    and the `stage_retried` payload records
                    `delegated: "infra_flake"`. Omitted → behavior
                    byte-identical to today.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Stage re-opened; refreshed Stage row in body.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Stage' }
        '400':
          description: |
            Malformed request body, or `override: true` without a
            non-empty `reason`. Body's `error` is `validation_failed`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            Possible error codes: (1) bearer token lacks both `write:stages`
            and `write:retries` scope — body `error` is `insufficient_scope`;
            (2) caller is an mcp token whose run scope does not match the
            target stage's `run_id` — body `error` is `cross_run_retry`.
            MCP tokens (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) may only retry stages
            within their own run. (3) `delegated: true` refusals
            (ADR-040 / #1026): `delegation_not_configured` when no
            effective `operator_agent` block delegates the retry action
            (fail-closed), `delegation_condition_unmet` when the
            `infra_flake` condition is not satisfied — `details` names
            the exact failed predicate in `unmet_reason`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '422':
          description: |
            Retry not applicable for the stage's current state /
            failure category. Body's `error` is `retry_not_applicable`
            and `message` explains the specific reason.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/stages/{stage_id}/fixup:
    post:
      operationId: fixupStage
      summary: Route advisory implement-review concerns back to the agent
      description: |
        Re-opens an implement stage held at the implement-review gate so
        the agent can make a bounded, operator-gated fix-up pass against one
        or more advisory implement-review concerns (E22.X / #762, #780),
        instead of the operator hand-editing the PR branch.

        Preconditions:
