        - the stage must be an **implement** stage (plan/review stages are
          not eligible). Otherwise 422 (`fixup_not_applicable`).
        - the run must not already be terminal
          (`succeeded`/`failed`/`cancelled`, #968): a completed run has no
          live gate to flow the fix-up back into, so its stages are never
          re-opened. Otherwise 422 (`fixup_not_applicable`).
        - the implement stage must be re-openable, in one of two flows.
          Otherwise 422 (`fixup_not_applicable`):
          - **commit-yourself**: the implement stage is its own gate, parked
            at `awaiting_approval`; or
          - **push_and_open_pr** (#780): the implement stage has
            `succeeded` (it committed and opened the PR) and the run's
            SEPARATE **review** stage is still parked at `awaiting_approval`
            (the PR is open, not merged). Refused once the review stage has
            merged/resolved, or when the run has no review stage.
        - on the deprecated positional path, the stage must carry a
          recorded **`approve_with_concerns`** implement-review verdict
          (the concern set the `concerns` indices address). With no
          recorded concerns, 422 (`fixup_not_applicable`). The
          `concern_ids` path resolves against the durable concern store
          instead (#964); an unknown, foreign, plan-stage, or non-open ID
          is 400 (`validation_failed`).

        On success the implement stage transitions to `pending`
        (`awaiting_approval → pending`, or `succeeded → pending` on the
        push_and_open_pr flow); on the push_and_open_pr flow the review
        stage is also re-parked `awaiting_approval → pending` so the
        re-dispatched implement flows back into a fresh review (the
        re-parked review stage id is recorded on the audit entry as
        `reparked_review_stage_id`). The orchestrator then walks
        `pending → dispatched` and fires `workflow_dispatch`, and a
        `stage_fixup_triggered` audit entry is written carrying the routed
        stable concern IDs (`concern_ids`, #964) or the deprecated selected
        indices, the resolved concern objects (read back by the
        prompt renderer to deliver them as binding instructions — the #558
        condition-delivery framing), and the bounded-pass receipt
        (`pass_ordinal`, `max_passes`, `remaining_budget`). On the
        `concern_ids` path the routed concerns are additionally marked
        `addressed_pending` in the durable concern store (best-effort,
        with `reason` as the `state_reason`). Returns 200 with
        the post-orchestrator stage (typically `dispatched`). Orchestrator
        failures are logged but don't fail the request — the stage stays at
        `pending` and an operator can re-fire Advance manually.

        **Distinct from `POST /v0/stages/{stage_id}/retry`:** retry re-opens
        a *failed* stage and regenerates a fresh diff; fix-up re-opens a
        *healthy* review gate, commits onto the SAME PR branch (the runner's
        rebase-from-remote shared-branch path), and UPDATES the existing PR
        rather than opening a new one.

        **Bounded + operator-gated.** The NORMAL bound defaults to ONE pass
        per stage, enforced server-side by counting prior
        `stage_fixup_triggered` audit entries (there is no dedicated column).
        Once that budget is spent a further attempt returns 422
        (`fixup_budget_exhausted`) — but the operator may grant ONE additional
        pass by setting `force_additional_pass: true`, hard-capped at **3
        total passes** per stage (#860). A forced pass is audited (a `forced`
        flag plus the operator `reason` on the `stage_fixup_triggered` entry).
        Once the hard ceiling is reached, even a forced attempt returns the
        DISTINCT 422 (`fixup_ceiling_reached`), whose `details` carries a
        `remediation` pointer: for a late CI/SAST finding, commit the fix on the
        run branch then `fishhawk_vouch_commit` it (operator/operator-agent
        token, NOT the run-bound `fhm_` token, which is rejected
        `run_token_forbidden`) so the operator commit clears the run's
        sole-writer lineage gate without breaking ADR-035 (#1068/#1044).
        Otherwise merge with a follow-up, or start a fresh run. The operator
        still owns the merge.
      tags: [stages]
      parameters:
        - { name: stage_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        description: |
          Selects what to route back to the agent: recorded implement-review
          concerns by stable concern ID (`concern_ids`, the primary scheme,
          #964) or by deprecated positional index (`concerns`), and/or a
          free-text `operator_concern` that needs no pre-existing review
          concern (#1311). `concern_ids` and `concerns` are mutually exclusive
          — supplying both is 400 (`validation_failed`). At least one of
          `concern_ids`, `concerns`, or `operator_concern` must be supplied —
          none at all is 400 (`validation_failed`).
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              additionalProperties: false
              properties:
                concern_ids:
                  type: array
                  minItems: 1
                  items:
                    type: string
                    format: uuid
                  description: |
                    PRIMARY addressing (#964): stable concern UUIDs to route
                    back to the agent (at least one), as surfaced by
                    `GET /v0/runs/{run_id}`'s `concerns.items[].id`. Each ID
                    must name an OPEN (`raised`/`addressed_pending`/
                    `reopened`) implement-stage concern of THIS stage — an
                    unknown, duplicate, foreign-run/-stage, plan-stage, or
                    already-resolved ID is rejected (400
                    `validation_failed`), so a plan-stage concern can never
                    route into an implement fix-up. Requires the concern
                    store (503 `fixup_unconfigured` otherwise).
                concerns:
                  type: array
                  minItems: 1
                  items:
                    type: integer
                    minimum: 0
                  deprecated: true
                  description: |
                    DEPRECATED positional fallback: indices of the recorded
                    implement-review concerns to route back to the agent (at
                    least one). The indices address the concern set in the
                    stage's `implement_reviewed` audit entry, in append order
                    flattened across reviewers — ambiguous once multiple
                    heterogeneous review entries exist per stage (#964);
                    prefer `concern_ids`. Only valid when `concern_ids` is
                    absent. An out-of-range or duplicate index is rejected
                    (400 `validation_failed`).
                reason:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional operator rationale, recorded on the
                    `stage_fixup_triggered` audit entry (and as the routed
                    concerns' `state_reason` on the `concern_ids` path).
                allow_create:
                  type: array
                  items:
                    type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional repo-relative paths this fix-up will CREATE
                    (#823). Each declared path is folded into the implement
                    stage's effective `scope.files` for THIS fix-up pass only
                    (never the persisted plan scope) so the runner stages the
                    net-new file instead of failing the created-out-of-scope
                    gate (category-B, #818). Any created file NOT declared here
                    still fails category-B — declaring a path widens the
                    legitimate set without reopening the silent-strip hole.
                    Entries must be repo-relative: an absolute path or one
                    containing `..` is rejected (400 `validation_failed`,
                    `field: allow_create`).
                force_additional_pass:
                  type: boolean
                  default: false
                  description: |
                    Bounded operator override (#860). When the NORMAL fix-up
                    budget is already spent (`fixup_budget_exhausted`) but a
                    concern still needs the agent, set this `true` to grant ONE
                    pass beyond the budget. Hard-capped at **3 total passes**
                    per stage; the forced pass is audited (a `forced` flag plus
                    `reason` on the `stage_fixup_triggered` entry). At the hard
                    ceiling even a forced attempt returns 422
                    (`fixup_ceiling_reached`). Default `false` preserves the
                    prior single-pass behaviour.
                delegated:
                  type: boolean
                  default: false
                  description: |
                    Opts the fix-up into the ADR-040 delegated-action
                    path (#1026). The server re-evaluates the
                    `operator_agent.may_route_fixup` condition
                    (`convergent_concerns`) against current run state at
                    action time: 403 `delegation_not_configured` when no
                    effective block delegates the route_fixup action
                    (fail-closed), 403 `delegation_condition_unmet` with
                    the named failed predicate when the implement-review
                    round is unsettled, a reviewer rejected, or no
                    concern is open. When met, the fix-up proceeds and
                    the `stage_fixup_triggered` payload records
                    `delegated: "convergent_concerns"`. Omitted →
                    behavior byte-identical to today.
                implement_model:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional operator/driver model override for THIS fix-up
                    pass (#1164). Empty (omitted) inherits the run's
                    already-resolved implement model — byte-identical to
                    today. A non-empty value is validated against the
                    deployment's per-adapter allowed-model set exactly as the
                    plan gate validates it: a model outside the run adapter's
                    configured allow-list is rejected (422
                    `fixup_invalid_model`); an empty/unconfigured allow-list
                    fails open (any model accepted). The effective model —
                    override or inherited — is pinned on the
                    `stage_fixup_triggered` audit entry at trigger time
                    (`fixup_model` / `fixup_model_source`) and read back when
                    the runner fetches the fix-up prompt, and is surfaced on
                    the run's `fixup_model` status field.
                operator_concern:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional free-text operator instruction routed back to the
                    agent with NO pre-existing review concern (#1311). It is the
                    sanctioned escape hatch for a REQUIRED external-check failure
                    that has no Fishhawk review concern — a CodeQL/SAST alert on
                    a clean approve-with-zero-concerns diff — or any operator
                    steer that is not an implement-review concern. When supplied
                    it is converted server-side into a synthetic
                    `[high/operator]` concern folded into the routed set, so it
                    rides the same budget / applicability / audit / prompt-render
                    path and reaches the agent as a binding instruction (#558
                    framing) on the run branch — keeping lineage sole-writer (no
                    foreign hand-commit that would trip ADR-035). It needs NO
                    recorded `approve_with_concerns` verdict: `operator_concern`
                    ALONE is admitted on a zero-concern gate-open stage (it does
                    NOT raise `fixup_not_applicable`). At least one of
                    `concern_ids`, `concerns`, or `operator_concern` is required.
                    A whitespace-only value, or one whose byte length exceeds
                    4000 (the renderer's per-section cap), is rejected (400
                    `validation_failed`, `field: operator_concern`) rather than
                    silently dropped or truncated. The raw text is recorded on
                    the `stage_fixup_triggered` audit entry as `operator_concern`.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Stage re-opened for the fix-up pass; refreshed Stage row in body.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Stage' }
        '400':
          description: |
            Malformed body, no selection at all (none of `concern_ids` /
            `concerns` / `operator_concern`), both `concern_ids` and
            `concerns` supplied, an out-of-range / duplicate concern index,
            an unknown / foreign / plan-stage / non-open `concern_id`, or a
            whitespace-only or over-length (>4000 bytes) `operator_concern`.
            Body's `error` is `validation_failed`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            Possible error codes: (1) bearer token lacks both `write:stages`
            and `write:fixups` scope — body `error` is `insufficient_scope`;
            (2) caller is an mcp token (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) whose run
            scope does not match the target stage's `run_id` — body `error`
            is `cross_run_fixup`. MCP tokens may only fix up stages within
            their own run. (3) `delegated: true` refusals (ADR-040 /
            #1026): `delegation_not_configured` when no effective
            `operator_agent` block delegates the route_fixup action
            (fail-closed), `delegation_condition_unmet` when the
            `convergent_concerns` condition is not satisfied — `details`
            names the exact failed predicate in `unmet_reason`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '422':
          description: |
            Fix-up not applicable. One of: the stage is the wrong type/state
            or carries no recorded `approve_with_concerns` concerns
            (`fixup_not_applicable`); the NORMAL bounded pass count is already
            spent and no override was requested (`fixup_budget_exhausted` —
            one more pass is available via `force_additional_pass: true`); or
            the hard ceiling of 3 total passes is reached and the override can
            no longer help (`fixup_ceiling_reached`, distinct from
            `fixup_budget_exhausted`; its `details` carry a `remediation`
            pointer to the operator commit-and-`fishhawk_vouch_commit` path for
            a late CI/SAST finding, #1068/#1044); or the resolved `implement_model`
            override is not in the deployment's per-adapter allow-list
            (`fixup_invalid_model`, #1164 — `details` carry the resolved
            `model`, `model_source`, and `adapter`). Body's `message` explains
            the specific reason.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/concerns/{concern_id}/waive:
    post:
      operationId: waiveConcern
      summary: Waive an open review concern with an audited reason
      description: |
        Transitions one OPEN review concern (`raised`,
        `addressed_pending`, or `reopened`) to the terminal `waived`
        state (E22.X / #984) — the operator judgment that the concern
        does not warrant a change (false positive, accepted trade-off,
        deliberate deferral), as distinct from routing it back to the
        agent via `POST /v0/stages/{stage_id}/fixup`.

        The `reason` is REQUIRED and audited. **Audit-before-mutation
        ordering:** the handler appends a `concern_waived` audit entry
        on the concern's run/stage FIRST (payload: `concern_id`,
        `prior_state`, `reason`, `stage_kind`, `severity`, `category`)
        and treats append failure as request failure (500
        `audit_append_failed`, no state change) — a waive mutation can
        never exist without a durable audit record. Only then is the
        state transition applied; if it fails after the append (a
        concurrent transition raced it), a corrective
        `concern_waive_failed` entry is appended (best-effort) naming
        the actual state and the request returns 422
        (`concern_waive_conflict`), keeping the chain truthful in every
        interleaving.

        Effects of a successful waive: the concern stops appearing in
        `GET /v0/runs/{run_id}`'s open-states-only `concerns` block, can
        no longer be selected by the fix-up endpoint's `concern_ids`,
        and is rendered in later implement-review prompts as
        not-re-litigable context carrying the operator's reason
        verbatim. The waive is terminal — there is no un-waive.

        Auth mirrors the fix-up endpoint: `write:stages` or
        `write:fixups` scope; an mcp run-bound token (subject
        `mcp:run:<uuid>`) may waive only its own run's concerns
        (403 `cross_run_waive` otherwise).
      tags: [stages]
      parameters:
        - { name: concern_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              additionalProperties: false
              required: [reason]
              properties:
                reason:
                  type: string
                  minLength: 1
                  description: |
                    REQUIRED operator rationale. Recorded on the
                    `concern_waived` audit entry, stored as the concern's
                    `state_reason`, and shown verbatim to later
                    re-reviews as the waive context — make it
                    self-contained.
                delegated:
                  type: boolean
                  default: false
                  description: |
                    Opts the waive into the ADR-040 delegated-action
                    path (#1026). The server re-evaluates the
                    `operator_agent.may_waive` condition (`solo_low`)
                    against current run state at action time, BEFORE the
                    `concern_waived` intent entry is appended: 403
                    `delegation_not_configured` when no effective block
                    delegates the waive action (fail-closed), 403
                    `delegation_condition_unmet` with the named failed
                    predicate unless exactly one concern is open and its
                    severity is low. When met, the waive proceeds and
                    the `concern_waived` payload records
                    `delegated: "solo_low"`. Omitted → behavior
                    byte-identical to today.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Concern waived; updated concern row in body.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/WaivedConcern' }
        '400':
          description: |
            Malformed body, missing/empty `reason`, or a non-UUID
            `concern_id`. Body's `error` is `validation_failed`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            Possible error codes: (1) bearer token lacks both `write:stages`
            and `write:fixups` scope — body `error` is
            `insufficient_scope`; (2) caller is an mcp token (subject
            `mcp:run:<uuid>`) whose run scope does not match the
            concern's `run_id` — body `error` is `cross_run_waive`.
            (3) `delegated: true` refusals (ADR-040 / #1026):
            `delegation_not_configured` when no effective
            `operator_agent` block delegates the waive action
            (fail-closed), `delegation_condition_unmet` when the
            `solo_low` condition is not satisfied — `details` names the
            exact failed predicate in `unmet_reason`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '422':
          description: |
            The concern is not in an open state — already `waived`,
            `superseded`, or `addressed`. Body's `error` is
            `concern_waive_conflict`; `details` carries the rejected
            `from`/`to` pair. A corrective `concern_waive_failed` audit
            entry accompanies the already-appended intent entry.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503':
          description: |
            Concern or audit store not configured on this deployment.
            Body's `error` is `concern_store_unconfigured`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/concerns/{concern_id}/defer:
    post:
      operationId: deferConcern
      summary: Defer an open review concern into a follow-up work item
      description: |
        Converts one OPEN review concern (`raised`, `addressed_pending`,
        or `reopened`) into a conventions-complete, boarded, epic-linked
        follow-up work item AND transitions the concern to the terminal
        `deferred` state in a single call (E22.X / #1202) — the operator
        verb that sits between routing the concern back to the agent
        (`POST /v0/stages/{stage_id}/fixup`) and resolving it with no
        follow-up (`POST /v0/concerns/{concern_id}/waive`). Deferring
        consumes NO fix-up budget.

        The follow-up body is fully AUTO-DRAFTED server-side from the
        persisted concern + its run (the concern note, severity,
        category, the reviewer model, the evidence run id, and the
        source PR link). The operator supplies only the non-derivable
        title coordinates — `parent_epic` (the epic the follow-up rolls
        up to) and `n` (the child number) — mirroring the work-item
        filing endpoint; `type` defaults to `bug` for a defect category
        else `chore`, and `labels` are merged on top of the type
        defaults.

        **Orphan-issue safety + audit-ordering:** a GitHub issue is a
        durable external side effect, so the handler (1) PRE-CHECKS the
        concern is open BEFORE any filing — the provider's File is never
        called for an already-resolved concern (422
        `concern_defer_conflict`, no issue created); (2) files the issue
        FIRST, then transitions the concern; a filing failure leaves the
        concern OPEN (no transition, no audit) so the operator can retry;
        (3) appends the success `concern_deferred` audit entry ONLY AFTER
        the transition succeeds (the entry is a fact, not an attempt). If
        the transition fails after a successful filing (a concurrent
        writer raced it), the ONLY audit entry is a corrective
        `concern_defer_failed` naming the actual state AND the orphaned
        issue url, and the request returns 422 — never a success
        `concern_deferred` entry for a transition that did not happen.

        Effects of a successful defer: the concern stops appearing in
        `GET /v0/runs/{run_id}`'s open-states-only `concerns` block, can
        no longer be selected by the fix-up endpoint's `concern_ids`, and
        its `state_reason` names the filed issue. The defer is terminal.

        Auth mirrors the waive endpoint: `write:stages` or `write:fixups`
        scope; an mcp run-bound token (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) may
        defer only its own run's concerns (403 `cross_run_defer`
        otherwise).
      tags: [stages]
      parameters:
        - { name: concern_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: false
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              additionalProperties: false
              properties:
                parent_epic:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    The epic the follow-up rolls up to (an issue
                    reference like `#1196`); its leading `[E<n>]` title
                    token is fetched to derive the `{epic}` title
                    placeholder. Operator judgment — not derivable from
                    the concern.
                n:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    The child number for the `[E<epic>.<n>]` title.
                    Operator judgment, mirroring how the work-item filing
                    endpoint takes `{n}` as a caller title var.
                type:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional override of the auto-selected work-item type
                    (`bug` for a defect category, else `chore`).
                labels:
                  type: array
                  items: { type: string }
                  description: Optional labels merged on top of the type's default labels.
                note:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional operator addendum folded into the follow-up
                    body and the concern's `state_reason`.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            Concern deferred; the filed follow-up issue + updated concern
            row in body.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/DeferredConcernResult' }
        '400':
          description: |
            Malformed body or a non-UUID `concern_id`. Body's `error` is
            `validation_failed`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            Possible error codes: (1) bearer token lacks both
            `write:stages` and `write:fixups` scope — body `error` is
            `insufficient_scope`; (2) caller is an mcp token (subject
            `mcp:run:<uuid>`) whose run scope does not match the
            concern's `run_id` — body `error` is `cross_run_defer`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '422':
          description: |
            Either the concern is not in an open state — already
            `waived`, `superseded`, `deferred`, or `addressed` (the
            open-state pre-check; no issue filed) — or the issue was
            filed but the state transition then failed (a post-filing
            race; a corrective `concern_defer_failed` audit entry names
            the orphaned issue url). Body's `error` is
            `concern_defer_conflict`. The auto-drafted follow-up could
            also violate the type's conventions — body `error` is
            `work_item_invalid` with `details`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '501':
          description: |
            The configured work-item provider is not registered. Body's
            `error` is `provider_unimplemented`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '502':
          description: |
            The provider could not file the follow-up issue — the
            concern stays OPEN (no transition). Body's `error` is
            `work_item_filing_failed`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503':
          description: |
            Concern, audit, or run store not configured on this
            deployment. Body's `error` is `concern_store_unconfigured`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/artifacts/{artifact_id}:
    get:
      operationId: getArtifact
      summary: Get an artifact
      tags: [artifacts]
      parameters:
        - { name: artifact_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Artifact with content.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Artifact' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Runner-facing endpoints
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  /v0/runs/{run_id}/signing-key:
    post:
      operationId: issueSigningKey
      summary: Issue a per-run Ed25519 signing key
      description: |
        Called by the runner at job start. The backend mints a fresh
        Ed25519 keypair, appends a new row in `signing_keys`, and
        returns the private half in the response body. The private key
        is never persisted server-side and never returned to anyone
        else. Auth is GitHub Actions OIDC token, verified per request.
        See ADR-008 (#72).

        Multi-call: each invocation appends a new row so each stage's
        fresh GitHub Actions runner process can issue its own key
        without coordinating with prior stages. `Verify` (used by the
        trace and plan upload endpoints) picks the latest unexpired
        key for the run; older rows remain so the standalone audit
        verifier can replay any signature that was valid at upload
        time. Migration 0012.

        TTL: the server sets `expires_at` to
        `max(30m, resolved_stage_budget + 5m)`, where
        `resolved_stage_budget` is the spec-governed timeout for the
        stage currently in `dispatched` or `running` state (sourced
        from `spec.ResolveStageTimeout`). This ensures a stage that
        runs its full budget still holds a valid key when it uploads
        its trace. Runs without a workflow spec, or with no active
        stage, default to 30m.
      tags: [runner]
      security:
        - githubOIDC: []
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: false
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                ttl_seconds:
                  type: integer
                  description: |
                    Optional override for the default TTL (1800s).
                    Backend may cap to a maximum.
                  minimum: 60
                  maximum: 3600
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Keypair issued.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                required: [run_id, public_key, private_key, issued_at, expires_at]
                properties:
                  run_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
                  public_key:
                    type: string
                    description: Base64-encoded 32-byte Ed25519 public key.
                  private_key:
                    type: string
                    description: |
                      Base64-encoded 64-byte Ed25519 private key. The
                      runner stores this in process memory only and
                      discards it after trace upload.
                  issued_at:
                    type: string
                    format: date-time
                  expires_at:
                    type: string
                    format: date-time
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '404':
          description: Run does not exist.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/trace:
    post:
      operationId: shipTrace
      summary: Upload a signed trace bundle
      description: |
        Called by the runner after a stage completes. The bundle bytes
        (`application/gzip`, JSON Lines) are uploaded as the request body;
        the signature comes via the `X-Fishhawk-Signature` header. The
        backend verifies the signature against the run's stored public
        key, stores the bundle in S3 (per ADR-003 #67), and writes the
        corresponding audit entry. Auth is the signature itself; no
        Bearer or cookie required.
      tags: [runner]
      security: []
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - { name: stage_id, in: query, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - name: variant
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [raw, redacted]
        - name: X-Fishhawk-Signature
          in: header
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
            description: |
              Hex-encoded 64-byte Ed25519 signature over
              sha256(raw_bundle_bytes).
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/gzip:
            schema:
              type: string
              format: binary
      responses:
        '202':
          description: Bundle accepted, signature verified.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                required: [run_id, stage_id, variant, content_hash]
                properties:
                  run_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
                  stage_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
                  variant: { type: string, enum: [raw, redacted] }
                  content_hash:
                    type: string
                    description: Hex sha256 of the uploaded bundle bytes.
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest' }
        '401':
          description: Signature invalid or no signing key for this run.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/plan:
    post:
      operationId: shipPlan
      summary: Upload a standard_v1 plan artifact
      description: |
        Called by the runner after a successful plan-stage agent
        invocation. The body is the agent-produced JSON validated
        against `docs/spec/plan-standard-v1.schema.json`. Auth is the
        per-run Ed25519 signature in `X-Fishhawk-Signature` (same
        scheme as `/v0/runs/{run_id}/trace`).

        Idempotent on (stage_id, content_hash): re-uploading the same
        plan bytes returns 200 with the existing artifact rather than
        inserting a duplicate row.
      tags: [runner]
      security: []
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - { name: stage_id, in: query, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - name: X-Fishhawk-Signature
          in: header
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
            description: |
              Hex-encoded 64-byte Ed25519 signature over
              sha256(plan_bytes).
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              description: Validated against plan-standard-v1.schema.json.
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Plan accepted; new artifact row created.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/PlanUploadResponse' }
        '200':
          description: |
            Plan with this content_hash already exists for the stage.
            The body returns the existing artifact unchanged (idempotent
            re-upload).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/PlanUploadResponse' }
        '400':
          description: |
            Body did not validate against `standard_v1` (`code: plan_invalid`)
            or path/query parameters were malformed.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401':
          description: Signature invalid or no signing key for this run.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '413':
          description: Plan body exceeds the per-stage size cap (256 KiB).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/pull-request:
    post:
      operationId: shipPullRequest
      summary: Upload a pull-request artifact for an implement stage
      description: |
        Called after an implement-stage run to report its commit/push/
        PR-open outcome. The body has three variants (#742, #771):

        - **Success**: a real GitHub PR was opened. The body carries
          `{ pr_number, pr_url, branch, head_sha, base_sha, title,
          body?, files_changed_count }`. The handler stores the artifact,
          backfills `run.pull_request_url`, and drives the implement
          stage's terminal transition when the trace handler's
          push-and-open-pr gate left it in `running`.
        - **Failure** (`{ outcome: "failed", category, reason }`): the
          commit/push/PR-open step failed, so no PR was opened. The
          handler fails the implement stage (category `C` is retryable,
          `B` parks for re-scope) instead of creating an artifact —
          closing the zombie-run hole where a commit/push failure
          stranded the run at review with a null PR.
        - **Child push** (`{ outcome: "pushed", branch, head_sha,
          base_sha, files_changed_count }`, #771): a decomposed child
          pushed onto the shared parent branch without opening a PR. The
          handler drives the child stage's terminal transition its
          push_to_shared_branch trace gate left in `running`, without
          creating a PR artifact — the decomposition-child analogue of the
          #742 zombie fix.

        Two auth paths are accepted:

        - **Ed25519 signature** (canonical runner path): supply
          `X-Fishhawk-Signature` — the hex-encoded 64-byte Ed25519
          signature over sha256(body), using the per-run ephemeral
          key issued at `/v0/runs/{run_id}/signing-key`. Same scheme
          as `/v0/runs/{run_id}/trace` and `/v0/runs/{run_id}/plan`.

        - **Bearer token** (local-runner CLI path): supply
          `Authorization: Bearer <token>` with `write:runs` scope.
          Used by the CLI wrapper (`autoOpenPR`) when the per-run
          Ed25519 signing key is unavailable — the key is consumed
          by the trace and plan uploads inside the runner subprocess
          and is not accessible to the outer CLI process.

        When both are present the signature path wins. The audit
        payload's `auth_method` field records `ed25519` or `bearer`
        to identify which path was taken.

        Returns 401 `signature_or_bearer_required` when neither is
        present.

        Idempotent on (stage_id, content_hash): re-running a runner
        job that pushed the same commits returns the existing
        artifact unchanged rather than inserting a duplicate.

        v0 has no formal JSON Schema for the body — required-field
        checks happen in the handler. v1+ may graduate this to a
        versioned `pull_request_v1` schema.
      tags: [runner]
      security: []
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - { name: stage_id, in: query, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - name: X-Fishhawk-Signature
          in: header
          required: false
          schema:
            type: string
            description: Hex-encoded 64-byte Ed25519 signature over sha256(body).
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/PullRequestArtifactBody' }
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Pull-request accepted; new artifact row created.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/PullRequestUploadResponse' }
        '200':
          description: |
            Either:
            - A pull-request artifact with this content_hash already
              exists for the stage (idempotent re-upload returns the
              existing artifact unchanged), or
            - the failure variant (`outcome: failed`) was accepted and
              the implement stage was transitioned to `failed` (#742), or
            - the child-push variant (`outcome: pushed`) was accepted and
              the decomposed-child implement stage was transitioned
              terminally (#771), or
            - the fix-up push variant (`outcome: fixup_pushed`) was accepted
              and the fix-up implement stage was transitioned terminally
              (#794), or
            - the fix-up no-changes variant (`outcome: fixup_no_changes`) was
              accepted and the fix-up implement stage was transitioned
              terminally, re-parking the review gate (#856), or
            - the scope-completeness park variant (`outcome: scope_park`) was
              accepted and the implement stage was parked in
              `awaiting_scope_decision` for an operator exempt-or-fail
              decision (#1231).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                oneOf:
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PullRequestUploadResponse'
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PullRequestFailureResponse'
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PullRequestChildPushResponse'
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PullRequestFixupPushResponse'
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PullRequestFixupNoChangesResponse'
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PullRequestScopeParkResponse'
        '400':
          description: |
            Body missing required fields (`code: pull_request_invalid`)
            — for the failure variant, an unknown `category` or missing
            `reason` — or path/query parameters were malformed.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401':
          description: |
            Auth failed. Possible codes:
            - `signature_invalid` — `X-Fishhawk-Signature` did not verify against the run's public key, or no signing key exists for this run.
            - `signature_or_bearer_required` — request included neither `X-Fishhawk-Signature` nor an authenticated bearer token with `write:runs` scope.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '413':
          description: Pull-request body exceeds the per-stage size cap (32 KiB).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/deployment:
    post:
      operationId: shipDeployment
      summary: Record a deployment artifact for a deploy stage
      description: |
        Records ADR-038's signed `deployment` artifact (E23.5 / #1385) —
        the governance record of a delegated release. Modeled on
        `POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/pull-request`: Fishhawk holds no deploy
        logic or credentials, so the external pipeline runs the deploy and
        the body reports its settled outcome.

        The body carries `{ environment, ref, external_run_url, outcome,
        rollback_handle? }`. `outcome` is one of `succeeded` / `failed` /
        `partial` / `rolled_back` (the ADR-038 deploy dispositions). The
        handler stores the artifact (`artifact.kind = deployment`) and
        writes a `deployment_outcome_recorded` chained audit entry. When the
        body additionally carries `rollback_action` (`initiated` /
        `completed`) it writes the matching
        `deployment_rollback_initiated` / `deployment_rollback_completed`
        entry as well. The deploy outcome surfaces on the run's living-anchor
        issue timeline.

        Two auth paths are accepted, mirroring the pull-request upload:

        - **Ed25519 signature** (canonical deploy-executor path): supply
          `X-Fishhawk-Signature` — the hex-encoded 64-byte Ed25519
          signature over sha256(body), using the per-run ephemeral key. The
          signature path is NOT scope-gated.
        - **Bearer token** (operator path): supply `Authorization: Bearer
          <token>` with BOTH `write:runs` AND `write:deploy` scope (ADR-038 /
          #1390 — the deploy ship is a governance write specific to the deploy
          gate). A token holding `write:runs` but missing `write:deploy` is
          rejected 403 `insufficient_scope` (`required_scope: write:deploy`).

        When both are present the signature path wins. The audit payload's
        `auth_method` field records `ed25519` or `bearer`.

        Returns 401 `signature_or_bearer_required` when neither is present.

        Idempotent on (stage_id, content_hash): a re-delivery of the same
        deployment record returns the existing artifact unchanged rather
        than inserting a duplicate or writing a second audit entry.

        v0 has no formal JSON Schema for the body — required-field checks
        happen in the handler.
      tags: [runner]
      security: []
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - { name: stage_id, in: query, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - name: X-Fishhawk-Signature
          in: header
          required: false
          schema:
            type: string
            description: Hex-encoded 64-byte Ed25519 signature over sha256(body).
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/DeploymentArtifactBody' }
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Deployment accepted; new artifact row created.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/DeploymentUploadResponse' }
        '200':
          description: |
            A deployment artifact with this content_hash already exists for
            the stage (idempotent re-upload returns the existing artifact
            unchanged).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/DeploymentUploadResponse' }
        '400':
          description: |
            Body missing required fields or malformed
            (`code: deployment_invalid`) — for example an absent
            `environment`/`ref`/`external_run_url`, a non-http
            `external_run_url`, an `outcome` outside the closed set, or an
            unrecognized `rollback_action` — or path/query parameters were
            malformed.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401':
          description: |
            Auth failed. Possible codes:
            - `signature_invalid` — `X-Fishhawk-Signature` did not verify against the run's public key, or no signing key exists for this run.
            - `signature_or_bearer_required` — request included neither `X-Fishhawk-Signature` nor an authenticated bearer token with `write:runs` scope.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '403':
          description: |
            Bearer token holds `write:runs` but is missing the deploy-specific
            `write:deploy` scope (`code: insufficient_scope`,
            `required_scope: write:deploy`, ADR-038 / #1390).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '413':
          description: Deployment body exceeds the per-stage size cap (32 KiB).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/deployment/rollback:
    post:
      operationId: rollbackDeployment
      summary: Re-dispatch a delegating deploy's rollback path
      description: |
        Operator-triggered rollback sub-action for a delegating deploy
        (E23.6 / #1386, ADR-038's `rolled_back` disposition). Fishhawk holds
        no prod credentials, so a rollback is just another delegating
        trigger: it re-dispatches the SAME external pipeline down its
        rollback path and records the rollback run handle.

        The endpoint owns only the **initiate** side. It reads the run's
        deploy stage's `executor.delegate` config from the cached workflow
        spec and, by target:

        - **github_actions** — fires a `workflow_dispatch` of the SAME
          `workflow_ref` carrying the Fishhawk correlation
          (`fishhawk_run_id` / `fishhawk_stage_id`) plus
          `fishhawk_rollback=true` as inputs, then best-effort resolves the
          rollback run id (the dispatch endpoint returns 204 with no body).
          Re-dispatching the same `workflow_ref` with a rollback input (rather
          than a separately configured `rollback_workflow_ref`) avoids a
          `workflow-v*` schema bump; a distinct rollback ref stays a deferred
          additive spec field.
        - **webhook** — POSTs a rollback payload (`fishhawk_rollback: true`)
          to the delegate URL.

        On a successful dispatch it writes a `deployment_rollback_initiated`
        chained audit entry carrying the rollback run handle — **distinct**
        from the initial deploy's `deployment_dispatched` handle — and
        refreshes the run's living-anchor issue comment.

        **Terminal completion is NOT recorded here.** Setting the
        `rolled_back` outcome and writing `deployment_rollback_completed`
        happen ONLY when the rollback run reaches terminal: the external
        rollback pipeline reports it by calling back into
        `POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/deployment` with
        `{ outcome: "rolled_back", rollback_action: "completed" }`, which
        persists the `rolled_back` deployment artifact (the durable carrier
        of the deploy outcome) and chains `deployment_rollback_completed`.
        This is the rollback terminal callback path and is uniform across
        both targets.

        **Preconditions.** The run must have a deploy stage in a terminal
        state (`succeeded` or `failed`); an in-flight stage has nothing to
        revert (refused 409 `deploy_not_settled`).

        **Authorization.** Operator-only — an authenticated bearer with BOTH
        `write:runs` AND `write:deploy` scope (ADR-038 / #1390; a rollback is
        never runner-initiated, so there is no Ed25519 signature path). A token
        holding `write:runs` but missing `write:deploy` is refused 403
        `insufficient_scope` (`required_scope: write:deploy`). A run-bound MCP
        token (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) is EXEMPT from the `write:deploy`
        requirement and may roll back ONLY its own run's deploy; another run is
        refused 403 (`cross_run_rollback`).
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '202':
          description: |
            Rollback re-dispatched; the rollback run handle is echoed back.
            Terminal completion (the `rolled_back` outcome +
            `deployment_rollback_completed`) is recorded later via the
            deployment callback.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  run_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
                  stage_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
                  target: { type: string, enum: [github_actions, webhook], description: 'The delegate target the rollback was re-dispatched against.' }
                  gha_run_id: { type: integer, format: int64, description: 'Resolved GitHub Actions rollback run id; omitted/0 when not yet resolvable.' }
                  external_run_url: { type: string, description: 'URL of the external rollback run (GHA run html_url, or the webhook delegate URL).' }
                  message: { type: string }
        '400':
          description: '`run_id` is not a valid UUID (`validation_failed`).'
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            One of: (1) bearer token lacks `write:runs` scope —
            `insufficient_scope`; (2) an operator bearer holds `write:runs` but
            lacks the deploy-specific `write:deploy` scope —
            `insufficient_scope` (`required_scope: write:deploy`, ADR-038 /
            #1390; mcp run-bound tokens are exempt); (3) an mcp run-bound token
            targeting another run — `cross_run_rollback`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404':
          description: |
            No run with that id (`run_not_found`), or the run has no deploy
            stage to roll back (`deploy_stage_not_found`).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '409':
          description: |
            The deploy stage has not reached a terminal outcome; nothing to
            roll back (`deploy_not_settled`).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '422':
          description: |
            The rollback could not be dispatched from config
            (`rollback_unconfigured`): the cached spec carries no delegating
            deploy stage, the github_actions delegate is missing a
            `workflow_ref`, the run has no `installation_id`, the webhook
            delegate is missing a `url`, or the repo ref is malformed.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '502':
          description: |
            The external rollback trigger failed (`rollback_dispatch_failed`):
            the `workflow_dispatch` returned non-204, or the webhook POST
            errored / returned a non-2xx status.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503':
          description: |
            Rollback unavailable (`rollback_unconfigured`): the backend has no
            run/audit repositories, or a github_actions rollback was requested
            against a backend with no configured GitHub client.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/installation-token:
    post:
      operationId: issueInstallationToken
      summary: Mint a GitHub App installation token for the run's repo
      description: |
        Returns the Fishhawk App's installation token for the
        repository the run is operating on. Lets the runner do its
        implement-stage `git push` and PR creation under the App's
        identity rather than the workflow's `GITHUB_TOKEN` — which
        means customers don't need to enable "Allow Actions to create
        and approve pull requests" in their repo settings, and the PR
        is correctly attributed to the App.

        Dual auth (#201):
        - **OIDC (canonical)** — `Authorization: Bearer <github-actions-oidc-jwt>`.
          Verified against the run row's repository + workflow
          claims via `cfg.OIDCVerifier`. Used by the auth pre-step
          before `actions/checkout` so the App token is what
          authenticates the initial clone too.
        - **Ed25519 (fallback)** — `X-Fishhawk-Signature: <hex>`
          over sha256 of the request body. Used by the runner's
          runtime fallback path when no auth pre-step ran (local
          dev, non-Actions environments).

        OIDC wins when both are present. Audit payload's
        `auth_method` field records which path was taken. Backend
        reads the run row's `installation_id` and calls the cached
        `githubapp.TokenProvider`; tokens are GitHub-default
        ~1 hour. Issuance is audited (`installation_token_issued`)
        with a sha256 of the token, never the token itself.
      tags: [runner]
      security: []
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - { name: stage_id, in: query, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - name: Authorization
          in: header
          required: false
          schema:
            type: string
            description: |
              `Bearer <github-actions-oidc-jwt>`. Required for the
              OIDC auth path. Mutually exclusive with
              X-Fishhawk-Signature; OIDC wins when both are sent.
        - name: X-Fishhawk-Signature
          in: header
          required: false
          schema:
            type: string
            description: |
              Hex-encoded 64-byte Ed25519 signature over sha256 of
              the request body (typically empty). Required for the
              Ed25519 auth path. Mutually exclusive with
              Authorization.
      requestBody:
        required: false
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              description: Empty in v0; reserved for future fields.
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Installation token issued.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                required: [token]
                properties:
                  token:
                    type: string
                    description: |
                      Opaque GitHub installation token. Use as
                      `x-access-token:<token>` for HTTPS git push,
                      or `Authorization: Bearer <token>` for REST
                      API calls. ~1-hour TTL; runner discards
                      after stage completes.
        '400':
          description: |
            run_id / stage_id malformed, stage doesn't belong to
            run, or the run has no `installation_id` (was created
            without a webhook-attributed installation).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401':
          description: Signature invalid or no signing key for this run.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '502':
          description: |
            Backend's App JWT was rejected, or the installation no
            longer exists. Runner should abort the implement stage
            and report category-C.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/mcp-token:
    post:
      operationId: issueMCPToken
      summary: Mint a short-lived MCP bearer token for the run
      description: |
        Per ADR-021 / #322 (resolved E19.1) the runner-side Claude
        Code agent gets a per-run, read-only bearer token to call
        the Fishhawk MCP server (`backend/cmd/fishhawk-mcp/`)
        mid-execution. This endpoint mints that token (E19.8 /
        #348).

        Token shape:
        - `fhm_` prefix + 32 random bytes URL-safe base64. Distinct
          from the operator-facing `fhk_` API tokens so the bearer-
          auth middleware can route to the right authenticator.
        - TTL: 60 minutes (load-bearing protection per ADR-021;
          per-endpoint scope enforcement lands as a separate
          ticket if needed).
        - Stored as sha256 in `mcp_tokens`; plaintext is shown
          exactly once on issue. Subject of `mcp:run:<run_id>` is
          stamped on requests authenticated with the token.

        Auth: Ed25519 signing-key path. The runner proves
        possession by signing the (empty) request body with the
        per-run private key it received from `signing-key`. Same
        Ed25519 shape `installation-token` uses; no OIDC fallback
        because this endpoint is runner-only (operators issue
        interactive MCP tokens via the `/v0/tokens` flow).

        Issuance is audited (`mcp_token_issued`). Payload carries
        the token id, expiry, TTL, and the prefix — never the
        plaintext bearer string.
      tags: [runner]
      security: []
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - name: X-Fishhawk-Signature
          in: header
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
            description: |
              Hex-encoded 64-byte Ed25519 signature over sha256 of
              the request body (typically empty).
      requestBody:
        required: false
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              description: Empty in v0; reserved for future fields.
      responses:
        '201':
          description: MCP token issued.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                required: [token, token_id, run_id, expires_at]
                properties:
                  token:
                    type: string
                    description: |
                      Plaintext bearer token. The runner stores this
                      in the agent's environment as
                      `FISHHAWK_API_TOKEN` and discards it. Shown
                      exactly once; no read path returns it.
                  token_id:
                    $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID'
                  run_id:
                    $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID'
                  expires_at:
                    type: string
                    format: date-time
        '400':
          description: |
            run_id is malformed.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401':
          description: |
            X-Fishhawk-Signature missing, malformed, or didn't
            verify against the run's stored public key.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '503':
          description: |
            The backend doesn't have the MCP token / run / signing
            repositories wired (dev backends running without the
            full set).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/scope-amendments:
    post:
      operationId: requestScopeAmendment
      summary: Request a mid-stage scope amendment (implement agent)
      description: |
        Files an operator-gated request to fold specific file paths
        into the executing implement stage's effective `scope.files`
        (E22.X / #961). The agent uses this instead of editing an
        undeclared file — the runner drops undeclared edits from the
        commit and fails category-B on undeclared created files
        (#818/#825).

        **Auth: run-bound `fhm_` bearer ONLY**, carrying the
        `write:scope-amendments` scope (granted unconditionally to
        implement-stage MCP tokens at issue). The path `run_id` must
        equal the token's run — a cross-run request is 403
        (`cross_run_scope_amendment`). Operator tokens/sessions are
        rejected (403 `agent_token_required`): operators decide,
        they don't request. The run's resolved active stage — the
        currently dispatched/running stage, or when none exists
        (e.g. an implement-only decomposition child whose stage is
        still `pending` under a local runner, #1030) the run's first
        non-terminal stage in sequence order — must be an implement
        stage (409 `stage_not_implement` otherwise).

        Budget: at most **2 requests per stage**, counted on rows so
        a denied request still consumes budget (the cap bounds
        operator interruptions, not approvals). Over budget → 422
        `amendment_budget_exhausted` with `{max, used}` details.

        The request lands as a `scope_amendment_requested` audit
        entry on the stage (`{amendment_id, paths, reason,
        remaining_budget}`) — the operator's `fishhawk_await_audit`
        anchor. Delivery is poll-based: the agent polls the GET
        until the status leaves `pending` (bounded ~5 minutes).
      tags: [runner]
      security: []
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [paths, reason]
              properties:
                paths:
                  type: array
                  minItems: 1
                  items:
                    type: object
                    required: [path, operation]
                    properties:
                      path: { type: string }
                      operation: { type: string, enum: [modify, create] }
                reason:
                  type: string
                  description: Why each path must change. Required, non-empty.
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Amendment request created (status `pending`).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ScopeAmendment' }
        '400':
          description: |
            Malformed run_id, invalid paths (absolute / `..` /
            unknown operation / empty), or empty reason.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401':
          description: No authenticated token.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '403':
          description: |
            `agent_token_required` (caller is not a run-bound token),
            `insufficient_scope` (token lacks
            `write:scope-amendments`), or
            `cross_run_scope_amendment` (token bound to a different
            run).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '409':
          description: |
            `stage_not_implement` — the run's resolved active stage
            (dispatched/running, else first non-terminal) is not an
            implement stage.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '422':
          description: |
            `amendment_budget_exhausted` — the per-stage cap of 2 is
            spent. Details carry `{max, used}`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503':
          description: Scope-amendment repositories not wired.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
    get:
      operationId: listScopeAmendments
      summary: List a run's scope amendments
      description: |
        Returns every scope amendment for the run, oldest first
        (E22.X / #961).

        **Auth: EITHER** the run-bound `fhm_` bearer with `mcp:read`
        — the implement agent's poll loop and the runner's pre-commit
        refresh both reuse the token the runner fetched at stage
        start, with the same path-`run_id` == token-run binding as
        the POST (cross-run → 403 `cross_run_scope_amendment`) —
        **OR** an operator bearer/session (not run-bound). Anonymous
        callers get 401.

        **Optional `?wait=<seconds>` long-poll (#1035).** When `wait`
        is a positive integer and at least one amendment is currently
        `pending`, the server holds the connection — re-listing
        internally — and returns early the moment any amendment that
        was pending at request time leaves `pending` (the
        honored-decision path), so a mid-stage decision is reachable
        while `fishhawk_run_stage` blocks the driving session. If no
        such decision lands within `wait` seconds, it returns the
        still-pending list at the cap and the client re-issues to
        extend toward its own total budget. `wait` is clamped to
        `0..30`; `0`, absent, or a non-positive/non-integer value is
        the unchanged single-list behavior (no wait). A `wait` with
        nothing currently pending returns immediately. The response
        envelope is unchanged — only the return timing differs; no new
        error codes.
      tags: [runner, runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - name: wait
          in: query
          required: false
          description: |
            Optional server-side long-poll in seconds (#1035). `0` or
            absent = no wait (unchanged single-list response). When
            positive and an amendment is pending, holds until a
            snapshotted-pending amendment is decided or the wait
            elapses; the client re-issues to extend. Clamped to
            `0..30`.
          schema:
            type: integer
            minimum: 0
            maximum: 30
            default: 0
      responses:
        '200':
          description: The run's amendments, oldest first.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                required: [items]
                properties:
                  items:
                    type: array
                    items: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ScopeAmendment' }
        '400':
          description: run_id is malformed.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401':
          description: No authenticated token.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '403':
          description: |
            `insufficient_scope` (run-bound token lacks `mcp:read`)
            or `cross_run_scope_amendment` (run-bound token reading
            another run's amendments).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503':
          description: Scope-amendment repository not wired.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/scope-amendments/{amendment_id}/decision:
    post:
      operationId: decideScopeAmendment
      summary: Approve or deny a scope amendment (operator)
      description: |
        Records the operator's decision on a pending scope amendment
        (E22.X / #961) and appends the `scope_amendment_decided`
        audit entry (`{amendment_id, decision, reason, decided_by}`).

        **Auth: operator bearer/session ONLY** — bearer tokens
        require `write:stages`. Run-bound `fhm_` tokens are rejected
        outright (403 `self_decision`): the requesting agent's token
        holds `write:scope-amendments` and must never decide its own
        request. Implement-stage tokens are never issued
        `write:stages`, so the run-bound rejection is defense in
        depth.

        On approve, the paths fold into the stage's effective
        `scope.files` at the next prompt fetch AND the runner's
        pre-commit refresh. Already-decided amendments return 409
        (`amendment_already_decided`) — decisions are immutable.
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - { name: amendment_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [decision]
              properties:
                decision:
                  type: string
                  enum: [approve, deny]
                reason:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Operator note; delivered to the agent verbatim on
                    deny (`decision_reason`), recorded on the audit
                    entry either way.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: The decided amendment.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ScopeAmendment' }
        '400':
          description: Malformed UUIDs or decision not approve/deny.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401':
          description: No authenticated token.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '403':
          description: |
            `self_decision` (run-bound agent token) or
            `insufficient_scope` (token lacks `write:stages`).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404':
          description: |
            `amendment_not_found` — unknown amendment id, or the
            amendment belongs to a different run than the path names.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '409':
          description: |
            `amendment_already_decided` — decisions are immutable.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503':
          description: Scope-amendment repositories not wired.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/scope-completeness/decision:
    post:
      operationId: decideScopeCompleteness
      summary: Exempt or fail a parked scope-completeness shortfall (operator)
      description: |
        Records the operator's exempt-or-fail decision on an implement
        stage parked in `awaiting_scope_decision` (#1231): the stage's
        ONLY committed-tree gate failure was the scope-completeness
        "missing declared scope file(s)" check, and the runner already
        pushed its gate-verified commit to the run branch (no PR).

        **Auth: operator bearer/session ONLY** — bearer tokens require
        `write:stages`. Run-bound `fhm_` tokens are rejected outright
        (403 `self_decision`): the implement agent that produced the
        parked commit must never decide its own exemption.

        On `exempt` the stage resumes `running` so the held commit's PR
        is opened with NO agent re-run (a `push_and_open_pr` dispatch
        opens the PR from the held commit), and a
        `scope_completeness_exempted` audit entry is appended. On `fail`
        the stage fails category-B (today's restore path) and a
        `scope_completeness_failed` entry is appended. A stage not
        currently parked returns 409 (`stage_not_parked`).
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [decision, reason]
              properties:
                decision:
                  type: string
                  enum: [exempt, fail]
                reason:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Operator note explaining the exemption or failure;
                    recorded on the audit entry. Required and non-empty.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            The resolved decision. On `exempt`, `held_commit_sha` /
            `run_branch` name the commit the PR is opened from.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                required: [stage_id, decision, state]
                properties:
                  stage_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
                  decision:
                    type: string
                    enum: [exempt, fail]
                  state:
                    type: string
                    description: The implement stage's resulting state.
                  held_commit_sha:
                    type: string
                    description: Present on `exempt` — the held commit the PR opens from.
                  run_branch:
                    type: string
                    description: Present on `exempt` — the run branch the held commit sits on.
        '400':
          description: Malformed run_id, decision not exempt/fail, or empty reason.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401':
